writingSC.stem: Only by working together __________ this goal.
writingSC.explanation_th: Only by... ขึ้นต้น → ผัน: can we achieve | we can achieve ไม่ผัน

writingSC.stem: The trip was exciting, educational, and __________.
writingSC.explanation_th: ขนานกับ adj (exciting, educational) → surprisingly cheap | อื่นไม่ใช่ adj

writingSC.stem: The author __________ is coming to our school next week.
writingSC.explanation_th: relative clause กรรม (ของ admire) → whom I admire most | which/what ผิด (ใช้กับคน)

writingSC.stem: __________ they will accept our offer is still unknown.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause เป็นประธาน (ความไม่แน่นอน) ใช้ Whether → Whether they will accept... | If ไม่ขึ้นต้นประธาน

writingSC.stem: __________ the news, the whole family began to celebrate.
writingSC.explanation_th: On + V-ing (ทันทีที่) → On hearing | On heard ผิดรูป, They heard = comma splice

writingSC.stem: The committee met __________ the new proposal.
writingSC.explanation_th: infinitive บอกจุดประสงค์ → to discuss | for discussing/discussing ผิด

writingSC.stem: He is used to __________ early every morning.
writingSC.explanation_th: be used to + V-ing → waking up | to wake up/wake up ผิด

writingSC.stem: She accused him __________ her idea.
writingSC.explanation_th: accuse somebody of + V-ing → of stealing | to steal/of steal ผิด

writingSC.stem: The shop offers great discounts; __________, the staff are very helpful.
writingSC.explanation_th: เพิ่มข้อมูล → in addition | however/nevertheless = ขัดแย้ง (ผิด)

writingSC.stem: Could you remind me __________?
options: what do I need to bring
what will I need to bring
writingSC.explanation_th: embedded question เรียงปกติ → what I need to bring | what do I... ผิด

writingSC.stem: __________ properly, this plant can live for decades.
writingSC.explanation_th: past participle ลดรูป passive (if it is cared for) → Cared for | Caring for = active (ผิด)

writingSC.stem: Today is __________ day of the year so far.
writingSC.explanation_th: superlative → the hottest | the hotter/hottest ไม่มี the/รูปผิด

writingSC.stem: If you had asked me, I __________ you the answer.
writingSC.explanation_th: เงื่อนไขที่ 3: would have + V3 → would have told | อื่นผิด tense

writingSC.stem: __________ that we discovered the hidden message.
writingSC.explanation_th: it-cleft เน้นเวลา: It was + ส่วนเน้น + that → It was only later that... | อื่นเรียงผิด

writingSC.stem: Physics, along with chemistry, __________ required for this program.
writingSC.explanation_th: ประธานหลัก Physics (เอกพจน์); along with... ไม่นับ → is | are/were ผิด SVA

writingSC.stem: No sooner __________ than the guests arrived.
writingSC.explanation_th: No sooner (negative) ขึ้นต้น → ผัน: had we sat down | we had sat down ไม่ผัน

writingSC.stem: The instructions could not have been __________.
writingSC.explanation_th: comparative (any + comparative) → any clearer | any clear/more clearer ผิดรูป

writingSC.stem: If it __________ raining, we would have gone hiking.
writingSC.explanation_th: เงื่อนไขที่ 3: if + had + V3 → had stopped | อื่นผิด tense

writingSC.stem: The road was closed for repairs; __________, we had to take a detour.
writingSC.explanation_th: เหตุ-ผล → as a result | however/in contrast = ขัดแย้ง (ผิด)

writingSC.stem: The summer __________ was unforgettable.
options: that we travelled across Europe on
writingSC.explanation_th: ขยายเวลา (summer) → when we travelled across Europe | which/that ขาดบุพบท

writingSC.stem: Listen! The teacher ____ the instructions, so please be quiet.
writingSC.explanation_th: Listen! = สัญญาณว่ากำลังเกิดขึ้นตอนนี้ ใช้ Present continuous → is giving

writingSC.stem: NET-SAT English tests ____ grammar and reading every year.
writingSC.explanation_th: every year = ข้อเท็จจริงที่เกิดประจำ ใช้ Present simple; include เป็น state verb ไม่ใช้ continuous

writingSC.stem: She has taught English in Kalasin ____ 2018.
writingSC.explanation_th: since ใช้กับจุดเวลาเริ่มต้น (a point in time) เช่น ปี / วันที่; for ใช้กับระยะเวลา เช่น for 5 years

writingSC.stem: The students ____ finished revising tenses; they are ready for the test now.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง have/has + just + V3 = เพิ่งเสร็จสิ้น → Present perfect; ขาด auxiliary จะเป็นประโยคไม่สมบูรณ์

writingSC.stem: The teacher ____ not ____ the test results yet.
writingSC.explanation_th: Present perfect negative: has/have + not + V3; yet ในปฏิเสธ = ยังไม่ได้ทำ → has not announced

writingSC.stem: Last year, he ____ the NET-SAT English exam and got an excellent score.
writingSC.explanation_th: last year = เวลาเจาะจงในอดีต → Past simple; Present perfect ใช้ไม่ได้กับ last year

writingSC.stem: It ____ heavily when we arrived at the test center.
writingSC.explanation_th: ฝนเป็นฉากหลังที่กำลังดำเนินอยู่ + when + arrived (past simple) → Past continuous

writingSC.stem: She ____ take both TGAT and NET-SAT English tests next year.
writingSC.explanation_th: แผนที่วางไว้ล่วงหน้าแล้ว (prior intention) → be going to + V1; 'is going' ขาด to

writingSC.stem: The NET-SAT English exam ____ at 9 a.m. tomorrow, so don't be late.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตาราง/กำหนดการที่ตายตัว (timetable) → Present simple แม้กล่าวถึงอนาคต

writingSC.stem: By the end of this week, I ____ all 100 tense questions.
writingSC.explanation_th: by the end of this week = เสร็จสิ้นก่อนถึงจุดในอนาคต → Future perfect

writingSC.stem: Phueanhan ____ this English exam style before, so she feels more confident.
writingSC.explanation_th: ประสบการณ์ในชีวิต + before → Present perfect negative; 'she feels' ยืนยันว่าเป็นปัจจุบัน

writingSC.stem: While the teacher ____ the answers, the students ____ their mistakes.
writingSC.explanation_th: สองกระบวนการเกิดพร้อมกันในอดีต + while → Past continuous ทั้งคู่

writingSC.stem: In 2022, the exam committee ____ some new grammar items in the TGAT English test.
writingSC.explanation_th: In 2022 = ปีเฉพาะที่จบแล้ว → Past simple; Present perfect ใช้กับ 'In 2022' ไม่ได้

writingSC.stem: She was nervous because she ____ any mock tests before the real exam.
writingSC.explanation_th: สาเหตุ (ไม่เคยทำ mock) เกิดก่อนผล (nervous ขณะสอบ) → Past perfect negative

writingSC.stem: She ____ English grammar for three hours, and she still isn't finished.
writingSC.explanation_th: กระบวนการต่อเนื่องจากอดีตถึงตอนนี้ (ยังไม่จบ) + for three hours → Present perfect continuous

writingSC.stem: By the time the examiner arrived, the students ____ all the instructions.
writingSC.explanation_th: By the time + past simple → Past perfect; เสร็จก่อน examiner มาถึง

writingSC.stem: They ____ for the TGAT English test all morning, and they are still not tired.
writingSC.explanation_th: all morning = ระยะเวลาต่อเนื่องถึงปัจจุบัน (ยังไม่จบ – still not tired) → Present perfect continuous

writingSC.stem: When I arrived at the exam hall, I realized I ____ my ID card at home.
writingSC.explanation_th: การลืมบัตรเกิดก่อนมาถึง (past before past) → Past perfect

writingSC.stem: He ____ harder than usual this week because the TGAT is coming soon.
writingSC.explanation_th: this week = ระยะเวลาต่อเนื่องหลายวัน → Present perfect continuous เน้น effort ที่ต่อเนื่อง

writingSC.stem: The exam ____ at 8 a.m. and ____ at 11 a.m.
options: is going to start, is going to finish
writingSC.explanation_th: ตารางสอบที่กำหนดตายตัว → Present simple (timetable usage)

writingSC.stem: By this time next week, we ____ our final English exam.
writingSC.explanation_th: by this time next week = จะเสร็จก่อนถึงเวลานั้น → Future perfect

writingSC.stem: She was confident because she ____ similar questions in last year's exam.
writingSC.explanation_th: การตอบคำถามปีที่แล้วเกิดก่อน was confident → Past perfect

writingSC.stem: By the end of this month, our class ____ all the tenses that can appear in the entrance exams.
writingSC.explanation_th: by the end of this month = จะเสร็จสิ้นก่อนสิ้นเดือน → Future perfect

writingSC.stem: The students ______ in the classroom at the moment.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ are studying – Present Continuous + SVA: 'at the moment' = signal คำ; The students = พหูพจน์ → are
✘ is studying – ผิด SVA (students → are)
✘ study – Present Simple ไม่เน้นขณะนี้
✘ studies – ผิด SVA และผิด tense

writingSC.stem: Neither my father nor my mother ______ well.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ cooks – SVA: Neither...nor → กริยาผันตามประธานที่ใกล้ that สุด = 'mother' (3rd singular) → cooks
✘ cook – ผิด SVA กับ mother
✘ are cooking – ผิด SVA; mother → is cooking
✘ have cooked – ผิด SVA; mother → has cooked

writingSC.stem: The police ______ investigating the case.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ are – SVA: 'police' เป็น collective noun ที่ใช้ plural verb เสมอ (ไม่มี 'a police')
✘ is – ผิด; police ไม่ใช่ singular
✘ was – Past tense และผิด SVA
✘ has been – ผิด SVA กับ police

writingSC.stem: The number of students ______ increasing.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – SVA: 'The number of + noun' → singular verb (หัวประธานคือ 'The number' ไม่ใช่ students)
✘ are – ผิด; students ไม่ใช่ประธานหลัก
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิด SVA กับ The number

writingSC.stem: A number of books ______ on the table.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ are – SVA: 'A number of + plural noun' → plural verb (A number of = several)
✘ is – ผิด; A number of ≠ The number of
✘ was – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ has been – ผิด SVA กับ A number of books

writingSC.stem: The committee ______ meeting tomorrow.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – SVA: Collective nouns (committee, team, family, group) ในภาษาอังกฤษอเมริกัน → singular verb
✘ are – British English usage ยอมรับได้ แต่ในบริบทนี้ใช้ singular
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิด SVA

writingSC.stem: Some of the students ______ absent today.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ are – SVA: 'Some of + countable plural noun' → plural verb (students เป็น plural)
✘ is – ผิด; Some of + plural → plural
✘ was – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ has been – ผิด SVA

writingSC.stem: Either you or he ______ wrong.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – SVA: Either...or → กริยาผันตามประธานที่ใกล้ที่สุด = 'he' (3rd singular) → is
✘ are – ผิด SVA; 'he' ไม่ใช่ plural
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิด SVA กับ he

writingSC.stem: I ______ him since we graduated.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ haven't seen – Present Perfect Negative: 'since' = signal; สถานะที่ต่อเนื่องถึงปัจจุบัน
✘ don't see – Present Simple ไม่แสดง connection กับ 'since'
✘ didn't see – Past Simple ตัด connection กับปัจจุบัน
✘ haven't been seeing – see (ความหมาย 'พบ') เป็น state verb ไม่ใช้ continuous

writingSC.stem: Some of the water ______ spilled.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – SVA: 'Some of + uncountable noun (water)' → singular verb
✘ are – ผิด; water เป็น uncountable → singular
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิด SVA

writingSC.stem: Five miles ______ a long distance to walk.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – SVA: ระยะทางที่แสดงเป็น unit เดียว → singular; 'Five miles' = หนึ่ง unit
✘ are – ผิด; แม้ดูเป็นพหูพจน์แต่เป็น unit เดียว
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิด SVA

writingSC.stem: The army ______ defending the city.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – Present Continuous + SVA: Collective noun 'army' → singular (American English)
✘ are – British English usage แต่ในบริบทนี้ใช้ singular
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิดกาล

writingSC.stem: The pair of shoes ______ under the bed.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – SVA: 'The pair of + noun' → ประธานหลักคือ 'pair' (singular) → is
✘ are – ผิด; shoes ไม่ใช่ประธานหลัก
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิด SVA

writingSC.stem: The population of the city ______ growing rapidly.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – SVA: 'The population of + noun' → ประธานหลักคือ 'population' (uncountable/singular) → is
✘ are – ผิด; city ไม่ใช่ประธานหลัก
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิด SVA

writingSC.stem: The army ______ defeated quickly.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ was – Past Simple Passive + SVA: Collective noun 'army' → singular; เหตุการณ์จบแล้วในอดีต
✘ is – Present tense ผิดกาล
✘ were – ผิด SVA (army → was)
✘ have been – ผิด SVA (army → has been)

writingSC.stem: Economics ______ a difficult subject.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is – SVA: -ics subjects (Economics, Physics, Statistics, Linguistics) → singular verb เสมอ
✘ are – ผิด; Economics ดูเหมือนพหูพจน์แต่เป็น singular discipline
✘ were – Past tense ผิดกาล
✘ have been – ผิด SVA

writingSC.stem: The news ______ all over the world.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ is spreading – Present Continuous + SVA: 'news' = uncountable singular → is; กำลังแพร่กระจายตอนนี้
✘ spread – Present Simple ไม่แสดงกระบวนการขณะนี้
✘ spreads – Present Simple + SVA ถูกบางส่วนแต่ไม่เน้น 'ขณะนี้'
✘ are spreading – ผิด SVA (news → is)

writingSC.stem: A number of people ______ waiting outside.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ are – SVA: 'A number of + plural noun' → plural verb (A number of = several/many)
✘ is – ผิด; A number of ≠ The number of
✘ was – Past tense และผิด SVA
✘ has been – ผิด SVA กับ A number of people

writingSC.stem: She ______ for the test all morning.
writingSC.explanation_th:
✔ has been studying – Present Perfect Continuous: 'all morning' = duration ตลอดช่วงเช้า ต่อเนื่องถึงตอนนี้
✘ studies – Present Simple บอกกิจวัตร
✘ is studying – Present Continuous ไม่แสดง duration ตั้งแต่เช้า
✘ studied – Past Simple ตัด connection กับปัจจุบัน

writingSC.stem: The emergency call __________ by the operator right now to prevent any further delay.
writingSC.explanation_th: right now = กำลังเกิด → Present Continuous Passive (is being + V3)

writingSC.stem: The urgent message regarding the policy changes __________ to all employees yesterday.
writingSC.explanation_th: yesterday = อดีต; ประธานหลัก message เอกพจน์ → Past Simple Passive (was + V3)

writingSC.stem: The final decision regarding the budget __________ before the annual meeting started.
writingSC.explanation_th: before + started (อดีต) → Past Perfect Passive (had been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The main door of the facility __________ by the security guards every evening.
writingSC.explanation_th: every evening; ประธานหลัก The main door เอกพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (is + V3)

writingSC.stem: The outer walls of the estate __________ while I was doing the homework inside.
writingSC.explanation_th: while + was doing = กำลังเกิดในอดีต; walls พหูพจน์ → Past Continuous Passive (were being + V3)

writingSC.stem: All confidential documents __________ via secure email before the upcoming deadline.
writingSC.explanation_th: อนาคต → Future Simple Passive (will be + V3); 'will submitted' ผิดไวยากรณ์

writingSC.stem: The rare flowers __________ by the gardener tomorrow morning between 8 and 9 AM.
writingSC.explanation_th: ช่วงเวลาเจาะจงในอนาคต = กำลังเกิด → Future Continuous Passive (will be being + V3)

writingSC.stem: The development plan __________ by the time the president makes a final decision.
writingSC.explanation_th: by the time + makes (future) → Future Perfect Passive (will have been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The large office windows __________ thoroughly every Sunday afternoon.
writingSC.explanation_th: every Sunday; windows พหูพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (are + V3)

writingSC.stem: The custom-made shirt __________ by the time the anniversary party ends tonight.
writingSC.explanation_th: by the time + ends (future) → Future Perfect Passive (will have been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The laboratory computer __________ by the skilled technician at the moment.
writingSC.explanation_th: at the moment = กำลังเกิด → Present Continuous Passive (is being + V3)

writingSC.stem: The multi-tiered wedding cake __________ specifically for the grand party last night.
writingSC.explanation_th: last night = อดีต → Past Simple Passive (was + V3)

writingSC.stem: The annual financial report __________ before the regional director arrived at the venue.
writingSC.explanation_th: before + arrived (อดีต) → Past Perfect Passive (had been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The standardized aptitude test __________ to all students every academic term.
writingSC.explanation_th: every academic term; test เอกพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (is + V3)

writingSC.stem: The suspension bridge __________ by engineers when the unexpected accident happened.
writingSC.explanation_th: when + happened (เหตุการณ์แทรก) = กำลังเกิดในอดีต → Past Continuous Passive (was being + V3)

writingSC.stem: The software development project __________ by the technical team last month.
writingSC.explanation_th: last month = อดีตเจาะจง → Past Simple Passive (was + V3)

writingSC.stem: The fragile packages __________ by the registered courier service tomorrow afternoon.
writingSC.explanation_th: tomorrow afternoon = อนาคต → Future Simple Passive (will be + V3)

writingSC.stem: The entire apartment house __________ by the sub-contracted workers at this time next week.
writingSC.explanation_th: at this time next week = กำลังเกิด ณ จุดอนาคต → Future Continuous Passive (will be being + V3)

writingSC.stem: All remaining admission tickets __________ by the end of the week due to high demand.
writingSC.explanation_th: by the end of = เสร็จก่อนจุดอนาคต → Future Perfect Passive (will have been + V3)

writingSC.stem: Fresh whole-wheat bread __________ by the local baker every single morning.
writingSC.explanation_th: every morning; bread นับไม่ได้/เอกพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (is + V3)

writingSC.stem: The urgent official email __________ by the general manager right now.
writingSC.explanation_th: right now = กำลังเกิด → Present Continuous Passive (is being + V3)

writingSC.stem: The introductory chemistry lesson __________ by a substitute teacher yesterday.
writingSC.explanation_th: yesterday = อดีต → Past Simple Passive (was + V3 ของ teach)

writingSC.stem: The vehicle's engine __________ thoroughly before the long cross-country trip started.
writingSC.explanation_th: before + started (อดีต) → Past Perfect Passive (had been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The spare keys to the laboratory __________ by the staff every single day.
writingSC.explanation_th: every day; ประธานหลัก keys พหูพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (are + V3)

writingSC.stem: The interior walls __________ by the decorators while the party was going on downstairs.
writingSC.explanation_th: while + was going = กำลังเกิดในอดีต; walls พหูพจน์ → Past Continuous Passive (were being + V3)

writingSC.stem: The assigned homework __________ by nine o'clock last night before she slept.
writingSC.explanation_th: by nine + before slept (อดีต) → Past Perfect Passive (had been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The official institutional website __________ by the technical unit by the end of next month.
writingSC.explanation_th: by the end of next month = เสร็จก่อนจุดอนาคต → Future Perfect Passive (will have been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The public television set __________ in the main hall every evening.
writingSC.explanation_th: every evening; TV set เอกพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (is + V3)

writingSC.stem: The action movie __________ at the cinema by the time you arrive at the lobby.
writingSC.explanation_th: start เป็น intransitive verb ใช้ Active เท่านั้น; by the time + arrive (future) → Future Perfect Active (will have + V3) — เป็นตัวลวงที่ออกสอบบ่อย

writingSC.stem: The conference rooms __________ with fresh flowers every weekend for the events.
writingSC.explanation_th: every weekend; rooms พหูพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (are + V3)

writingSC.stem: The main computer system __________ when I left the office building last night.
writingSC.explanation_th: when + left (เหตุการณ์แทรก) = กำลังเกิดในอดีต → Past Continuous Passive (was being + V3)

writingSC.stem: The safety instructions __________ clearly to all foreign participants before the race.
writingSC.explanation_th: before the race (อดีต); instructions พหูพจน์ → Past Simple Passive (were + V3)

writingSC.stem: The registration form __________ correctly before submission to the officer.
writingSC.explanation_th: Modal Passive = modal + be + V3 → must be filled

writingSC.stem: Nutritious organic food __________ in the kitchen by our chefs daily.
writingSC.explanation_th: daily; food นับไม่ได้/เอกพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (is + V3)

writingSC.stem: The textbook __________ by the teacher while the students were reading quietly.
writingSC.explanation_th: while + were reading = กำลังเกิดในอดีต → Past Continuous Passive (was being + V3)

writingSC.stem: The ancient tree __________ by the fierce storm last night near the highway.
writingSC.explanation_th: last night = อดีต; tree เอกพจน์ → Past Simple Passive (was + V3)

writingSC.stem: All admission tickets __________ completely before the official concert show started.
writingSC.explanation_th: before + started (อดีต) → Past Perfect Passive (had been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The old property __________ by the real estate agent by the end of the year.
writingSC.explanation_th: by the end of the year = เสร็จก่อนจุดอนาคต → Future Perfect Passive (will have been + V3)

writingSC.stem: The downtown streets __________ thoroughly every evening by the public cleaners.
writingSC.explanation_th: every evening; streets พหูพจน์ → Present Simple Passive (are + V3)

writingSC.stem: The complex technical problem __________ by our engineers by the time the boss arrives.
writingSC.explanation_th: by the time + arrives (future) → Future Perfect Passive (will have been + V3)

writingSC.stem: __________ he had prepared thoroughly, he still felt nervous before the presentation.
writingSC.explanation_th: เตรียมดีแต่ยังประหม่า = ความขัดแย้ง → although

writingSC.stem: The project was delayed __________ unexpected technical issues.
writingSC.explanation_th: because of + noun phrase

writingSC.stem: She saved money __________ she could travel abroad.
writingSC.explanation_th: แสดงจุดประสงค์ (purpose)

writingSC.stem: He didn't understand the instructions; __________, he asked for help.
writingSC.explanation_th: แสดงผลลัพธ์

writingSC.stem: __________ the high cost, many people still bought the product.
writingSC.explanation_th: despite + noun phrase

writingSC.stem: You won't succeed __________ you work harder.
writingSC.explanation_th: unless = if not

writingSC.stem: __________ she was exhausted, she finished the report on time.
writingSC.explanation_th: ความขัดแย้ง

writingSC.stem: He invested a lot; __________, he gained little profit.
writingSC.explanation_th: ผลตรงข้ามความคาดหมาย

writingSC.stem: __________ being inexperienced, she handled the task well.
writingSC.explanation_th: despite + gerund

writingSC.stem: The meeting was canceled __________ the manager was absent.
writingSC.explanation_th: เหตุผล + ประโยค

writingSC.stem: He studied late into the night __________ he could pass the exam.
writingSC.explanation_th: purpose clause

writingSC.stem: She didn't like the movie; __________, she stayed until the end.
writingSC.explanation_th: contrast

writingSC.stem: __________ his lack of experience, he was hired.
writingSC.explanation_th: despite + noun phrase

writingSC.stem: He will join us __________ he finishes his work.
writingSC.explanation_th: after = time conjunction

writingSC.stem: The weather was terrible; __________, the event continued as planned.
writingSC.explanation_th: nevertheless = contrast ระดับ formal

writingSC.stem: __________ she had little time, she completed the task efficiently.
writingSC.explanation_th: contrast

writingSC.stem: He missed the train __________ he woke up late.
writingSC.explanation_th: cause

writingSC.stem: __________ working overtime, he couldn't finish the project.
writingSC.explanation_th: despite + gerund

writingSC.stem: She practiced daily __________ she could improve her skills.
writingSC.explanation_th: purpose

writingSC.stem: He was warned; __________, he ignored the advice.
writingSC.explanation_th: contrast

writingSC.stem: __________ the fact that he was tired, he continued working.
writingSC.explanation_th: despite the fact that = สำนวนตายตัว

writingSC.stem: The company expanded __________ the economic downturn.
writingSC.explanation_th: despite + noun

writingSC.stem: __________ she had studied, she failed the exam.
writingSC.explanation_th: contrast

writingSC.stem: He improved significantly __________ practicing regularly.
writingSC.explanation_th: because of + gerund (practicing)

writingSC.stem: She left early __________ she could catch the bus.
writingSC.explanation_th: purpose

writingSC.stem: __________ his efforts, he did not succeed.
writingSC.explanation_th: despite + noun phrase

writingSC.stem: He didn't attend the meeting; __________, he sent a report.
writingSC.explanation_th: instead = แสดงการแทนที่

writingSC.stem: __________ she is young, she is very capable.
writingSC.explanation_th: contrast

writingSC.stem: He worked hard; __________, he achieved his goals.
writingSC.explanation_th: result

writingSC.stem: __________ the noise, I couldn't sleep.
writingSC.explanation_th: because of + noun (the noise)

writingSC.stem: __________ he had no experience, he was confident.
writingSC.explanation_th: contrast

writingSC.stem: She didn't study; __________, she failed.
writingSC.explanation_th: result (เหตุ→ผลที่คาดได้)

writingSC.stem: __________ being sick, he went to work.
writingSC.explanation_th: despite + gerund

writingSC.stem: He stayed home __________ it was raining.
writingSC.explanation_th: cause

writingSC.stem: __________ the deadline was near, they worked faster.
writingSC.explanation_th: cause

writingSC.stem: She kept going __________ she was exhausted.
writingSC.explanation_th: contrast

writingSC.stem: He didn't call; __________, I called him.
writingSC.explanation_th: result

writingSC.stem: __________ the bad weather, the flight was delayed.
writingSC.explanation_th: because of + noun phrase

writingSC.stem: She studied hard; __________, she passed easily.
writingSC.explanation_th: result

writingSC.stem: __________ he tried hard, he failed.
writingSC.explanation_th: contrast

writingSC.stem: My brother, __________ lives abroad, will visit us soon.
writingSC.explanation_th: non-defining clause + คน → who

writingSC.stem: The house, __________ roof is red, is beautiful.
writingSC.explanation_th: whose = แสดงความเป็นเจ้าของ (roof of the house)

writingSC.stem: Bangkok, __________ I was born, is crowded.
writingSC.explanation_th: where = แทนสถานที่

writingSC.stem: 2020, __________ the pandemic started, changed everything.
writingSC.explanation_th: when = แทนเวลา

writingSC.stem: The reason __________ he left is unclear.
writingSC.explanation_th: reason + why

writingSC.stem: The woman, __________ I met yesterday, is a singer.
writingSC.explanation_th: กรรม + non-defining (comma)

writingSC.stem: The city, __________ is very large, attracts tourists.
writingSC.explanation_th: non-defining + สิ่งของ

writingSC.stem: The man, __________ car was stolen, reported to police.
writingSC.explanation_th: whose = แสดงเจ้าของ (car of the man)

writingSC.stem: The school, __________ I studied, is famous.
writingSC.explanation_th: where = สถานที่

writingSC.stem: The day __________ we met was special.
writingSC.explanation_th: when = เวลา

writingSC.stem: The hotel, __________ we stayed, was luxurious.
writingSC.explanation_th: where = สถานที่

writingSC.stem: The author, __________ books are popular, will visit.
writingSC.explanation_th: whose = แสดงเจ้าของ (books of the author)

writingSC.stem: The event, __________ was canceled, disappointed many.
writingSC.explanation_th: non-defining + สิ่งของ

writingSC.stem: The time __________ we arrived was late.
writingSC.explanation_th: when = เวลา

writingSC.stem: The reason __________ she cried is unknown.
writingSC.explanation_th: reason + why

writingSC.stem: The village, __________ he grew up, is quiet.
writingSC.explanation_th: where = สถานที่

writingSC.stem: The man, __________ I spoke to, was kind.
writingSC.explanation_th: กรรม (I spoke to him)

writingSC.stem: The car, __________ engine is broken, stopped.
writingSC.explanation_th: whose = แสดงเจ้าของ (engine of the car)

writingSC.stem: The museum, __________ we visited, was huge.
writingSC.explanation_th: กรรมของ visited + สิ่งของ

writingSC.stem: The place __________ we met is closed.
writingSC.explanation_th: where = สถานที่

writingSC.stem: The year __________ he graduated was 2022.
writingSC.explanation_th: when = เวลา

writingSC.stem: The person, __________ you called, is busy.
writingSC.explanation_th: กรรมของ called

writingSC.stem: The building, __________ was tall, collapsed.
writingSC.explanation_th: non-defining + สิ่งของ

writingSC.stem: The boy, __________ bike was stolen, cried.
writingSC.explanation_th: whose = แสดงเจ้าของ (bike of the boy)

writingSC.stem: The restaurant, __________ we ate, was nice.
writingSC.explanation_th: where = สถานที่

writingSC.stem: The night __________ we met was unforgettable.
writingSC.explanation_th: when = เวลา

writingSC.stem: The reason __________ they failed is clear.
writingSC.explanation_th: reason + why

writingSC.stem: The company, __________ he works for, is large.
writingSC.explanation_th: กรรมของ works for + สิ่งของ

writingSC.stem: The house, __________ we bought, is old.
writingSC.explanation_th: กรรมของ bought + สิ่งของ

writingSC.stem: The park, __________ is nearby, is clean.
writingSC.explanation_th: non-defining + สิ่งของ

writingSC.stem: I enjoy _ to music while I study.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง enjoy ต้องใช้ gerund (V-ing) เสมอ → listening; enjoy ไม่ตามด้วย to-infinitive

writingSC.stem: She admitted _ the homework.
writingSC.explanation_th: admit + V-ing = ยอมรับว่าทำ → copying

writingSC.stem: They kept _ even after the teacher entered the room.
writingSC.explanation_th: keep + V-ing = ทำต่อเนื่อง → talking

writingSC.stem: He is interested in _ architecture.
writingSC.explanation_th: interested in + V-ing เพราะ in เป็น preposition → studying

writingSC.stem: We are looking forward to _ you again.
writingSC.explanation_th: look forward to + V-ing; to ตรงนี้เป็น preposition ไม่ใช่ to-infinitive → meeting

writingSC.stem: She apologized for _ late.
writingSC.explanation_th: apologize for + V-ing → arriving

writingSC.stem: I have difficulty _ this chapter.
writingSC.explanation_th: have difficulty + V-ing → understanding

writingSC.stem: They decided against _ the trip because of the rain.
writingSC.explanation_th: decide against + V-ing (against เป็น preposition) → postponing

writingSC.stem: He is used to _ early every morning.
writingSC.explanation_th: be used to + V-ing/noun = คุ้นเคยกับ → waking

writingSC.stem: There is no point in _ about the past.
writingSC.explanation_th: there is no point in + V-ing → worrying

writingSC.stem: She avoided _ him in the hallway.
writingSC.explanation_th: avoid + V-ing → seeing

writingSC.stem: I can't help _ when I see that video.
writingSC.explanation_th: can't help + V-ing = อดไม่ได้ที่จะ → laughing

writingSC.stem: He finished _ the report before 5 p.m.
writingSC.explanation_th: finish + V-ing → writing

writingSC.stem: Do you mind _ the window?
writingSC.explanation_th: mind + V-ing → opening

writingSC.stem: She suggested _ the meeting until next week.
writingSC.explanation_th: suggest + V-ing → delaying

writingSC.stem: I'm not good at _ maps.
writingSC.explanation_th: good at + V-ing (at เป็น preposition) → reading

writingSC.stem: He confessed to _ the answer from his friend.
writingSC.explanation_th: confess to + V-ing (to เป็น preposition) → getting

writingSC.stem: The machine needs _ carefully.
writingSC.explanation_th: need + V-ing = ต้องได้รับการ (มีความหมาย passive) → checking

writingSC.stem: She gave up _ coffee last month.
writingSC.explanation_th: give up + V-ing → drinking

writingSC.stem: We spent hours _ for the test.
writingSC.explanation_th: spend time/hours + V-ing → preparing

writingSC.stem: He insists on _ the truth.
writingSC.explanation_th: insist on + V-ing (on เป็น preposition) → telling

writingSC.stem: I'm tired of _ the same mistake.
writingSC.explanation_th: be tired of + V-ing → making

writingSC.stem: The students were busy _ posters for the event.
writingSC.explanation_th: be busy + V-ing → designing

writingSC.stem: She's not afraid of _ in public.
writingSC.explanation_th: afraid of + V-ing (of เป็น preposition) → speaking

writingSC.stem: He denied _ the document.
writingSC.explanation_th: deny + V-ing → signing

writingSC.stem: We are thinking about _ a new car.
writingSC.explanation_th: think about + V-ing → buying

writingSC.stem: The child is fond of _ cartoons.
writingSC.explanation_th: fond of + V-ing → watching

writingSC.stem: They talked about _ to another school.
writingSC.explanation_th: talk about + V-ing → transferring

writingSC.stem: She is capable of _ the work on time.
writingSC.explanation_th: capable of + V-ing → finishing

writingSC.stem: I remember _ him at the station.
writingSC.explanation_th: remember + V-ing = จำได้ว่าเคยทำในอดีต → meeting

writingSC.stem: He is interested in _ a doctor someday.
writingSC.explanation_th: interested in + V-ing → becoming

writingSC.stem: After _ dinner, we watched a movie.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง after (preposition) ใช้ V-ing → eating

writingSC.stem: She's good at _ difficult problems.
writingSC.explanation_th: good at + V-ing → solving

writingSC.stem: He prevented me from _ the wrong door.
writingSC.explanation_th: prevent someone from + V-ing → opening

writingSC.stem: The boy admitted _ the vase.
writingSC.explanation_th: admit + V-ing → breaking

writingSC.stem: They are accustomed to _ with strict rules.
writingSC.explanation_th: be accustomed to + V-ing (to เป็น preposition) → working

writingSC.stem: She is thinking of _ abroad next year.
writingSC.explanation_th: think of + V-ing → studying

writingSC.stem: He has trouble _ at night.
writingSC.explanation_th: have trouble + V-ing → sleeping

writingSC.stem: There's no use _ him again.
writingSC.explanation_th: there's no use + V-ing → calling

writingSC.stem: The teacher recommended _ the article first.
writingSC.explanation_th: recommend + V-ing → reading

writingSC.stem: She kept _ the same question.
writingSC.explanation_th: keep + V-ing → asking

writingSC.stem: I'm used to _ by myself.
writingSC.explanation_th: be used to + V-ing → studying

writingSC.stem: He succeeded in _ the scholarship.
writingSC.explanation_th: succeed in + V-ing → winning

writingSC.stem: They missed _ the train by two minutes.
writingSC.explanation_th: miss + V-ing = พลาด/ไม่ทัน → catching

writingSC.stem: I can't stand _ in a crowded bus.
writingSC.explanation_th: can't stand + V-ing = ทนไม่ได้ → waiting

writingSC.stem: She objected to _ the bill alone.
writingSC.explanation_th: object to + V-ing (to เป็น preposition) → paying

writingSC.stem: He is busy _ the room for the guests.
writingSC.explanation_th: be busy + V-ing → cleaning

writingSC.stem: They are considering _ to Japan.
writingSC.explanation_th: consider + V-ing → moving

writingSC.stem: I'm excited about _ my results.
writingSC.explanation_th: excited about + V-ing → getting

writingSC.stem: She objected to _ her plans in public.
writingSC.explanation_th: object to + V-ing → discussing

writingSC.stem: If I ____ the answer, I would tell you.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 2 Conditional: สมมติปัจจุบัน Trick: หลายคนเห็น 'would tell' แล้วเผลอเลือก future แต่จริงๆ if-clause ต้องเป็น past simple

writingSC.stem: If she had studied medicine, she ____ a doctor now.
writingSC.explanation_th: Mixed Conditional: อดีตต่างไป ผลปัจจุบันต่างไป Trick: คำว่า 'now' บอกผลปัจจุบัน แต่ if-clause เป็นอดีตสมมติ จึงต้องใช้ 'would be'

writingSC.stem: Had I known about the meeting, I ____ there on time.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Inversion: Had I known = If I had known Trick: ข้อสอบชอบตัด If ออกเพื่อดูว่ารู้ไหมว่าเป็นอดีตสมมติ ต้องจับคู่กับ would have + V3

writingSC.stem: We can go hiking as long as it ____ sunny.
writingSC.explanation_th: Conditional Marker (as long as): เงื่อนไข 'ตราบใดที่' ใช้ Present Simple Trick: แม้เหมือนเป็นอนาคต แต่โครงสร้างเงื่อนไขในอนุประโยคต้องเป็น present

writingSC.stem: Take your umbrella in case it ____.
writingSC.explanation_th: Conditional Marker (in case): หลัง in case ใช้ Present Simple Trick: คนชอบใส่ 'will rain' เพราะแปลว่า 'เผื่อว่าฝนจะตก' แต่ตามกฎห้ามใช้ 'will' ตามหลัง

writingSC.stem: If I ____ more confident, I would speak in public more often.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 2 Conditional แบบสมมติ: 'were' ใช้ได้กับทุกประธานในรูปทางการ Trick: ระวังถูกหลอกระหว่าง was/were โดยเฉพาะกับประธาน I/he/she/it

writingSC.stem: If he had taken the medicine, he ____ better by now.
writingSC.explanation_th: Mixed Conditional: อดีตต่างไป ผลปัจจุบันต่างไป Trick: คำว่า 'by now' บอกผลลัพธ์ในปัจจุบัน จึงไม่ใช้ would have been

writingSC.stem: She would have bought the house if she ____ enough money.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Conditional: เหตุการณ์ในอดีตที่ไม่เกิดขึ้นจริง (If + Past Perfect) Trick: main clause เป็น would have bought จึงต้องใช้ had + V3 (had had) ใน if-clause

writingSC.stem: ____ I known the traffic was this bad, I would have left earlier.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Inversion: Had I known = If I had known Trick: เป็นการตัด If ออกแล้วสลับที่ประธานกับกริยาช่วย โครงสร้างยังเป็นอดีตสมมติ

writingSC.stem: You can use my laptop provided that you ____ careful with it.
writingSC.explanation_th: Conditional Marker (provided that): เงื่อนไขจำกัด 'ก็ต่อเมื่อ' ตามด้วย Present Simple Trick: เห็นผลลัพธ์เป็นสิ่งที่จะเกิด อย่าเผลอใส่ 'will' ใน clause เงื่อนไข

writingSC.stem: We’ll start the presentation in case the manager ____.
writingSC.explanation_th: Conditional Marker (in case): ตามด้วย Present Simple เสมอ Trick: หลอกให้เลือก 'will arrive' เพราะในภาษาไทยแปลว่า 'เผื่อว่าเขาจะมา'

writingSC.stem: If they had listened carefully, they ____ the instructions.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Conditional: if-clause เป็น Past Perfect (had listened) ผลลัพธ์จึงต้องเป็น would have + V3

writingSC.stem: Unless you ____ now, you’ll miss the bus.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 1 Conditional: unless = if not ทำหน้าที่เหมือน Type 1 Trick: หลัง unless ต้องใช้ Present Simple

writingSC.stem: If she ____ the truth yesterday, she would have reacted differently.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Conditional: สมมติอดีต Trick: 'yesterday' บังคับให้เป็นเรื่องในอดีต และ main clause ก็บ่งชี้ผลลัพธ์สมมติในอดีต (would have reacted) จึงต้องใช้ Past Perfect

writingSC.stem: I would travel more if I ____ more free time.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 2 Conditional: สมมติปัจจุบัน main clause ใช้ 'would + V1' ดังนั้น if-clause ต้องเป็น Past Simple

writingSC.stem: If the prices had not increased, more people ____ the product.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Conditional: เงื่อนไขอดีต (had not increased) นำไปสู่ผลลัพธ์สมมติในอดีต (would have bought)

writingSC.stem: If I ____ you, I would accept the offer.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 2 Conditional: สมมติสิ่งที่ไม่จริงในปัจจุบัน โครงสร้าง If I were... เป็นรูปแบบมาตรฐานที่นิยมนำมาออกสอบ

writingSC.stem: If the team had trained harder, they ____ the championship.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Conditional: เงื่อนไขสมมติในอดีตที่ไม่เป็นจริง (If + Past Perfect, would have + V3)

writingSC.stem: He will call you as soon as he ____.
writingSC.explanation_th: Time Clause: 'as soon as' ใช้ Present Simple เพื่อสื่อความหมายถึงอนาคต Trick: คล้าย If-clause เมื่อผลลัพธ์เป็นอนาคต ประโยคเงื่อนไขเวลาจะเป็นปัจจุบัน

writingSC.stem: You may leave early provided that your work ____ finished.
writingSC.explanation_th: Conditional Marker (provided that): ทำหน้าที่คล้าย If ในเงื่อนไขที่เป็นจริง ต้องตามด้วย Present Simple

writingSC.stem: If they had left earlier, they wouldn’t ____ the traffic jam.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Conditional: รูปปฏิเสธของ would have + V3 คือ wouldn't have + V3 Trick: หลัง wouldn't ต้องเป็น have (infinitive) เสมอ

writingSC.stem: As long as the documents ____ complete, the application will be accepted.
writingSC.explanation_th: Conditional Marker (As long as): ความหมายเดียวกับ If แบบเป็นจริงในอนาคต ต้องใช้ Present Simple ตามหลังเสมอ

writingSC.stem: If she had practiced more, she ____ the competition.
writingSC.explanation_th: Type 3 Conditional: อดีตสมมติที่ไม่เกิดขึ้นจริง If-clause เป็น Past Perfect ผลลัพธ์จึงเป็น would have + V3

writingSC.stem: The laboratory space in the new campus is ____ larger than the old one.
writingSC.explanation_th: larger เป็นขั้นกว่าอยู่แล้ว คำขยายเพื่อเน้นย้ำขั้นกว่าได้คือ much, far ห้ามใช้ very หรือ too นำหน้าขั้นกว่า

writingSC.stem: This simulation program is by ____ the best tool for statistical analysis.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง 'by far + the + ขั้นสูงสุด' เป็นสำนวนใช้ขยายขั้นสูงสุดเพื่อบอกว่า 'ที่สุดแบบขาดลอย'

writingSC.stem: The newly built conference hall is twice ____ large as our previous auditorium.
writingSC.explanation_th: การเปรียบเทียบเป็นเท่าตัวคือ 'X times + as + adj. + as'

writingSC.stem: The ____ the data set, the more reliable the conclusion becomes.
writingSC.explanation_th: Double Comparative (ยิ่ง...ยิ่ง...) รูปแบบคือ 'The + ขั้นกว่า..., the + ขั้นกว่า...'

writingSC.stem: The inflation rate this quarter is significantly ____ than predicted by economists.
writingSC.explanation_th: significantly ขยายขั้นกว่า มีคำใบ้ 'than' จึงต้องตอบ lower ห้ามใช้ more ซ้อนกับ -er

writingSC.stem: Her approach was far ____ than the one used in the pilot study.
writingSC.explanation_th: Far ทำหน้าที่ขยายขั้นกว่า มีคำว่า than จึงต้องใช้ more effective

writingSC.stem: Of all the revisions, the final draft was by far the ____.
writingSC.explanation_th: 'Of all' เป็นการเปรียบเทียบขั้นสูงสุด 'the clearest' ถูกต้องที่สุด

writingSC.stem: The security systems of modern banks are far ____ advanced than those used a decade ago.
writingSC.explanation_th: far ขยายขั้นกว่า (far more + adj.) โจทย์มี advanced และ than จึงเติมเพียง more

writingSC.stem: The higher the consumer demand for the product, ____ the market price rises.
writingSC.explanation_th: ประโยคเงื่อนไขผกผันคู่ขนาน เมื่อครึ่งแรกเป็น 'The higher' ครึ่งหลังต้องเป็น 'the higher' เช่นกัน

writingSC.stem: The written test was not nearly ____ challenging as the oral examination.
writingSC.explanation_th: สำนวน 'not nearly as + adj. + as' แปลว่า 'เทียบกันไม่ได้เลย'

writingSC.stem: Purchasing software licenses in bulk is a ____ cheaper than buying them individually.
writingSC.explanation_th: สำนวน 'a lot + ขั้นกว่า' (cheaper) เพื่อบอกว่าถูกกว่ามาก หลอกตาด้วย much ที่ไม่ต้องมี 'a' นำหน้า

writingSC.stem: Among the three proposals, the second was the ____ adopted by the board.
writingSC.explanation_th: 'Among the three' บ่งบอกถึงขั้นสูงสุด จึงต้องใช้ easiest

writingSC.stem: The results were ____ more convincing after the sample size increased.
writingSC.explanation_th: 'far' ขยายขั้นกว่าได้ ส่วน 'farther' ใช้กับระยะทาง

writingSC.stem: Using public transportation can be much ____ efficient during peak traffic hours.
writingSC.explanation_th: much ใช้ขยายคุณศัพท์ขั้นกว่า บริบทนี้ต้องใช้ more efficient

writingSC.stem: The farther the researcher extended the analysis, the ____ the interpretation became.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง Double Comparative (The + ขั้นกว่า..., the + ขั้นกว่า...)

writingSC.stem: The sudden natural disaster made the regional supply chain logistics even ____.
writingSC.explanation_th: even ใช้ขยายขั้นกว่าแปลว่า 'ยิ่ง...เข้าไปใหญ่' ขั้นกว่าของ bad คือ worse

writingSC.stem: The professor owns at least twice as ____ books as his students do.
writingSC.explanation_th: books เป็นนามนับได้พหูพจน์ ในโครงสร้าง as...as จึงใช้ many

writingSC.stem: The construction costs for the tunnel are substantially ____ than the original budget.
writingSC.explanation_th: substantially (อย่างมาก) ขยายขั้นกว่า มีคำว่า than จึงตอบ higher

writingSC.stem: The medical board exam is by far ____ demanding test in the curriculum.
writingSC.explanation_th: by far ใช้เน้นย้ำขั้นสูงสุด ต้องตามด้วย the most demanding

writingSC.stem: The new procedure is ____ efficient than the old one, though it requires more training.
writingSC.explanation_th: มีคำว่า than ต้องใช้ comparative ของ efficient คือ more efficient

writingSC.stem: The automation process requires considerably ____ manual labor than before.
writingSC.explanation_th: labor (แรงงาน) เป็นคำนามนับไม่ได้ ในโครงสร้างขั้นกว่าใช้ less

writingSC.stem: The more carefully the manuscript was edited, the ____ the reviewers became about its argument.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้างคู่ขนาน The + ขั้นกว่า..., the + ขั้นกว่า...

writingSC.stem: The novel's sequel proved to be no ____ interesting than the original masterpiece.
writingSC.explanation_th: สำนวน 'no less + adj. + than' แปลว่า 'ไม่ได้น้อยไปกว่ากันเลย'

writingSC.stem: The sub-office file server is half ____ large as the primary database storage.
writingSC.explanation_th: การเปรียบเทียบสัดส่วนครึ่งหนึ่งใช้โครงสร้าง 'half + as + adj. + as'

writingSC.stem: The earlier the interview began, the ____ the candidate felt before entering the room.
writingSC.explanation_th: Double Comparative โครงสร้าง The + ขั้นกว่า..., the + ขั้นกว่า...

writingSC.stem: Volunteering at the orphanage was the ____ rewarding experience of her life.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำใบ้ 'of her life' และ 'the' บอกว่าเป็นบริบทขั้นสูงสุด

writingSC.stem: The internship program offers many ____ opportunities than expected.
writingSC.explanation_th: many ใช้นำหน้าคุณศัพท์บอกปริมาณขั้นกว่า (more + นามพหูพจน์) เพื่อแปลว่า 'มากกว่าเดิมอย่างมาก'

writingSC.stem: The junior developer proved to be just as ____ as the senior lead programmer.
writingSC.explanation_th: สำนวน 'just as + adj. + as' รูปด้านในต้องใช้คุณศัพท์ขั้นปกติ

writingSC.stem: The new government regulations are far ____ restrictive than the previous policies.
writingSC.explanation_th: มี far ขยายขั้นกว่า restrictive เป็นคำยาว ความหมายคือ 'เข้มน้อยลง' จึงใช้ less

writingSC.stem: The older the professor gets, ____ profound his academic lectures become.
writingSC.explanation_th: ประโยคยิ่ง...ยิ่ง... (Double Comparative) บังคับขึ้นต้นด้วย The ทั้งสองท่อน

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun-clause word order.
options: I wonder how does she manage her time so well.
I wonder how she manages her time so well.
I wonder how she manage her time so well.
I wonder how she managed so her time well.
writingSC.explanation_th: ใน noun clause โครงสร้างภายในต้องเป็นรูป statement: “how she manages her time” ไม่ใช่รูปคำถามตรง

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has the correct structure of a noun clause?
options: The fact that he is late to the meeting is very important.
The fact which he is late to the meeting is very important.
The fact that he is late the meeting to is very important.
The fact is very important that he is late to the meeting.
writingSC.explanation_th: “that he is late to the meeting” เป็น noun clause appositive ขยาย “the fact” โครงสร้าง that + S + V + prepositional phrase

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct academic syntax and clause connector.
options: Although the weather was bad, but the event was still held.
Although the weather was bad, the event was still held.
Although the weather was bad, the event was still held, although.
The event was still held although the weather was bad, that.
writingSC.explanation_th: “Although” เป็น subordinating conjunction นำ dependent clause แล้วตามด้วย main clause โดยไม่ใช้ “but” คู่กัน

writingSC.stem: Which sentence correctly uses a noun clause with “whether”?
options: Please tell me whether can you come or not.
Please tell me whether you can come or not.
Please tell me can you come or not.
Please whether tell me you can come or not.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause นำด้วย “whether” โครงสร้างภายในต้องเป็น statement: “whether you can come or not”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct relative clause structure.
options: The book which I borrowed from the library is very informative.
The book which I from the library borrowed is very informative.
The book which I borrowed from the library is very informative one.
The book which I borrowed very informative from the library is.
writingSC.explanation_th: relative clause “which I borrowed from the library” มีโครงสร้าง S + V + prepositional phrase ที่สมบูรณ์

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has the correct structure with a noun clause as the subject?
options: That students need to revise regularly is clear.
That students regularly need to revise is clear.
Students that need to revise regularly that is clear.
Clear is that students need to revise regularly.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause “That students need to revise regularly” เป็น subject + main verb “is” + complement “clear”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct academic word order and connector.
options: Because he was sick, therefore he did not attend the lecture.
Because he was sick, he did not attend the lecture.
He was sick because, he did not attend the lecture.
Because he did not attend the lecture, he was sick.
writingSC.explanation_th: “Because” เป็น subordinator สร้าง dependent clause แล้วตามด้วย main clause โดยไม่ต้องเติม “therefore”

writingSC.stem: Which sentence correctly uses a noun clause after “suggest”?
options: The teacher suggested that students should study more.
The teacher suggested that should students study more.
The teacher suggested students should study more that.
The teacher suggested study more students that.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง “suggest” ตามด้วย noun clause ขึ้นต้น “that” + subject + modal/verb: “that students should study more”

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has the correct structure of a noun clause as object?
options: I do not understand why he left the company so suddenly.
I do not understand why left he the company so suddenly.
I do not understand why he company left the so suddenly.
I do not understand why suddenly he left the company.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause เริ่มด้วย “why” โครงสร้าง subject + verb + adverb: “why he left the company so suddenly”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct relative clause word order.
options: The project which the students were working on was very difficult.
The project which the students working were on the was very difficult.
The project which the students were were working on very difficult.
The project which the students were working on very was difficult.
writingSC.explanation_th: relative clause “which the students were working on” ใช้โครงสร้าง S + were + verb + preposition ที่ถูกต้อง

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause word order.
options: The professor explained what are the main causes of climate change.
The professor explained what the main causes of climate change are.
The professor explained what the main are causes of climate change.
The professor explained are the main causes of climate change what.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause นำด้วย “what” ต้องเป็นรูป statement: “what the main causes of climate change are”

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has correct noun clause structure?
options: The reason why he failed the exam is because he did not study.
The reason why he failed the exam is that he did not study.
The reason that he failed the exam why is he did not study.
The reason he failed the exam is why he did not study.
writingSC.explanation_th: “that he did not study” เป็น noun clause ทำหน้าที่ subject complement ของ “The reason…”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct academic syntax and connector.
options: Because the data was unreliable, therefore the results were not accepted.
Because the data was unreliable, the results were not accepted.
The data was unreliable because, the results were not accepted.
The results were not accepted because the data was unreliable, therefore.
writingSC.explanation_th: “Because” นำ dependent clause แล้วตามด้วย main clause โดยไม่ใช้ “therefore” คู่กัน

writingSC.stem: Which sentence correctly uses a noun clause after “expect”?
options: We expect that can he finish the task on time.
We expect that he can finish the task on time.
We expect he can that finish the task on time.
We expect can he finish the task on time that.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง “expect” ตามด้วย that-clause รูป statement: “that he can finish the task on time”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause as subject.
options: How the students will solve the problem is still unknown.
How the students will solve is still unknown the problem.
The problem how will solve the students is still unknown.
How will the students solve the problem is still unknown.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause “How the students will solve the problem” เป็น subject + “is still unknown”

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has correct relative clause structure?
options: The hotel which we stayed in was very comfortable.
The hotel which we in stayed was very comfortable.
The hotel which we stayed was very comfortable in.
The hotel which in we stayed was very comfortable.
writingSC.explanation_th: relative clause “which we stayed in” ใช้โครงสร้าง S + verb + preposition โดยวาง preposition ท้าย clause

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause after “assume”?
options: The manager assumes why the report is late.
The manager assumes that the report is late.
The manager assumes the report is late that.
The manager assumes is late the report that.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง “assume” ตามด้วย that-clause รูป statement: “that the report is late”

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has correct academic word order with a connector?
options: Since the policy has changed, therefore the application process is simpler.
Since the policy has changed, the application process is simpler.
The policy has changed since, the application process is simpler.
The application process is simpler since the policy has changed, therefore.
writingSC.explanation_th: Since เป็น subordinator นำ dependent clause แล้วตามด้วย main clause โดยไม่ใช้ “therefore” คู่กัน

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause after “explain”?
options: The teacher explained how did the experiment work.
The teacher explained how the experiment worked.
The teacher explained worked the experiment how.
The teacher explained how worked the experiment.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause นำด้วย “how” ใช้โครงสร้าง statement: “how the experiment worked”

writingSC.stem: Which sentence correctly uses a noun clause as object with “doubt”?
options: I doubt that he will arrive on time.
I doubt that will he arrive on time.
I doubt that on time will he arrive.
I doubt he will arrive on time that.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง “doubt” ตามด้วย that-clause รูป subject + verb: “that he will arrive on time”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause structure.
options: The teacher informed us that will the test be postponed.
The teacher informed us that the test would be postponed.
The teacher informed us that the test be postponed would.
The teacher informed us would the test be postponed that.
writingSC.explanation_th: ใน that-clause ใช้โครงสร้าง subject + verb (would be postponed) ตามรูป statement

writingSC.stem: Which sentence correctly uses a noun clause as object?
options: I wonder if can he finish the report today.
I wonder if he can finish the report today.
I wonder if finish can he the report today.
I wonder he can finish the report today if.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง “I wonder” ใช้ if-clause รูป subject + verb: “if he can finish the report today”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct academic syntax and connector.
options: As the evidence strongly supports the theory, therefore the journal accepted the paper.
As the evidence strongly supports the theory, the journal accepted the paper.
As the evidence strongly supports the theory, the journal accepted the paper, therefore.
The journal accepted the paper as the evidence strongly supports the theory, therefore.
writingSC.explanation_th: “As” เป็น subordinator นำ dependent clause แล้วตามด้วย main clause โดยไม่ใช้ “therefore” คู่กัน

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has correct noun clause after “discover”?
options: They discovered what did cause the error.
They discovered what caused the error.
They discovered what the error caused.
They discovered what caused the error that.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause นำด้วย “what” ใช้โครงสร้าง statement: “what caused the error”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause as subject.
options: What the students need to learn is clearly explained.
What the students need to learn explained clearly is.
What the students need is clearly explained to learn.
What clearly explained is the students need to learn.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause “What the students need to learn” เป็น subject + “is clearly explained”

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has correct relative clause structure?
options: The proposal which the committee rejected was very ambitious.
The proposal which the committee rejected was very ambitious that.
The proposal which the committee was rejected was very ambitious.
The proposal which rejected the committee was very ambitious.
writingSC.explanation_th: relative clause “which the committee rejected” ใช้โครงสร้าง S + verb ที่ถูกต้อง

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause after “decide”?
options: They decided that will they attend the conference.
They decided that they would attend the conference.
They decided that they attend the conference would.
They decided that the conference would they attend.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง “decide” ตามด้วย that-clause รูป subject + verb (would attend…)

writingSC.stem: Which sentence correctly uses a noun clause after “suspect”?
options: The police suspect who stole the money.
The police suspect who the thief stole the money.
The police suspect who stole the money is.
The police suspect who the money was stolen by.
writingSC.explanation_th: “who stole the money” เป็น noun clause object ของ “suspect” โครงสร้าง S + V + who-clause

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct academic word order and connector.
options: Since the findings were inconclusive, however the study was published.
Since the findings were inconclusive, the study was published.
The study was published since the findings were inconclusive, however.
Since the findings were inconclusive, nevertheless the study was published.
writingSC.explanation_th: “Since” นำ dependent clause แล้วตามด้วย main clause โดยไม่ต้องใช้ conjunctive adverb คู่กัน

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has correct noun clause after “remember”?
options: I don’t remember when did the meeting start.
I don’t remember when the meeting started.
I don’t remember when started the meeting.
I don’t remember the meeting started when.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause นำด้วย “when” ใช้โครงสร้าง statement: “when the meeting started”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause structure.
options: The researcher explained how did the data support the hypothesis.
The researcher explained how the data supported the hypothesis.
The researcher explained how the data supported did the hypothesis.
The researcher explained how did supported the data the hypothesis.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause นำด้วย “how” ใช้โครงสร้าง statement: “how the data supported the hypothesis”

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has correct noun clause as object?
options: We assume that the results will be published soon.
We assume that will the results be published soon.
We assume the results will be published soon that.
We assume will be published the results soon that.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง “assume” ตามด้วย that-clause รูป subject + verb: “that the results will be published soon”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct academic syntax and connector.
options: Because the funding was reduced, the project was cancelled, however.
Because the funding was reduced, the project was cancelled.
The project was cancelled because the funding was reduced, however.
However the project was cancelled because the funding was reduced.
writingSC.explanation_th: “Because” นำ dependent clause แล้วตามด้วย main clause โดยไม่ใช้ conjunctive adverb คู่กัน

writingSC.stem: Which sentence has correct noun clause after “argue”?
options: The professor argues that the theory is incomplete.
The professor argues that is the theory incomplete.
The professor argues incomplete is the theory that.
The professor argues that incomplete the theory is.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง “argue” ตามด้วย that-clause รูป subject + verb: “that the theory is incomplete”

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct noun clause as subject.
options: Why the students failed the exam is not clear.
Why the students failed is not clear the exam.
The exam why failed the students is not clear.
Why failed the students the exam is not clear.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause “Why the students failed the exam” เป็น subject + “is not clear”

writingSC.stem: The university is conducting a long-term ______ of urban pollution levels.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง a long-term ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า investigation (-tion) คือ noun ของ investigate

writingSC.stem: The results of the study were ______ important for future policy decisions.
writingSC.explanation_th: were ตามด้วย adverb คำว่า significantly (-ly) ขยายระดับความสำคัญ

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new law has been widely discussed in academic circles.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า effect หมายถึง “ผลกระทบ” ส่วน effective คือ adjective

writingSC.stem: The scientist’s ______ data provided strong evidence for the theory.
writingSC.explanation_th: ก่อนคำนาม data ต้องการ past participle/adjective คำว่า collected (ข้อมูลที่ได้รับการเก็บรวบรวมแล้ว)

writingSC.stem: The government announced a new ______ program to reduce traffic accidents.
writingSC.explanation_th: program ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า safety หมายถึง “ความปลอดภัย”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the experiment depends on careful control of all variables.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า success แปลว่า “ความสำเร็จ” ส่วน successful คือ adjective

writingSC.stem: The article discusses the ______ influence of social media on political opinions.
writingSC.explanation_th: influence คือ noun จึงต้องการ adjective คำว่า significant หมายถึง “สำคัญ, มีนัยสำคัญ”

writingSC.stem: The committee approved the ______ of the new curriculum next semester.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง the ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า adoption (-tion) หมายถึง “การนำเข้าใช้”

writingSC.stem: The ______ rate of the program has increased steadily over the years.
writingSC.explanation_th: rate ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า participation (-tion) แปลว่า “การมีส่วนร่วม”

writingSC.stem: The research shows a clear ______ between economic growth and education levels.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง a clear ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า relation (-tion) แปลว่า “ความสัมพันธ์”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new rule will be explained at the next meeting.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า introduction (-tion) หมายถึง “การนำเข้าใช้”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the data has improved the reliability of the study.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า analysis (-sis) หมายถึง “การวิเคราะห์”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new technology is expected to change many industries.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า development (-ment) หมายถึง “การพัฒนา”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new policy has been widely debated in the media.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า implementation (-tion) หมายถึง “การนำนโยบายไปปฏิบัติ”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the program will be measured by the number of graduates.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า success แปลว่า “ความสำเร็จ” ส่วน successful คือ adjective

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new tax law has reduced income inequality.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า application (-tion) หมายถึง “การนำกฎไปใช้”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the research lies in its original methodology.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า significance (-ce) หมายถึง “ความสำคัญ”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new vaccine has brought hope to many families.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า discovery แปลว่า “การค้นพบ”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new drug has not yet been fully understood.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า effect คือ “ผลกระทบ/ผลลัพธ์” ส่วน affect เป็น verb

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the report was written by the team leader.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า conclusion (-sion) หมายถึง “บทสรุป”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new policy has raised many concerns among citizens.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า introduction (-tion) หมายถึง “การนำนโยบายเข้ามาใช้”

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new technology is clearly visible in everyday life.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า influence แปลว่า “อิทธิพล” ส่วน influential คือ adjective

writingSC.stem: The ______ of the new system has significantly improved efficiency.
writingSC.explanation_th: หลัง The ต้องการคำนาม คำว่า introduction (-tion) หมายถึง “การนำระบบใหม่เข้ามาใช้”

writingSC.stem: ____ student in the class must submit the report by Friday.
writingSC.explanation_th: student เป็นเอกพจน์ ใช้ each + singular noun เพื่อสื่อว่าทุกคนแต่ละคน ไม่ใช่ all ที่ต้องใช้กับพหูพจน์

writingSC.stem: ____ of the two proposals is acceptable, so we need to choose carefully.
writingSC.explanation_th: of the two แสดงว่ามีสองทางเลือก ใช้ either (ตัวใดตัวหนึ่ง) หรือ neither (ไม่ตัวใดเลย) ที่นี่สื่อว่าทั้งสองใช้ได้ เลยใช้ either

writingSC.stem: ____ of the answers was correct, so the teacher had to explain again.
writingSC.explanation_th: of the answers ใช้กับ neither เมื่อสื่อว่าไม่มีคำตอบใดถูกเลย ต่างจาก either ที่สื่อว่าตัวใดตัวหนึ่งถูก

writingSC.stem: The committee found that ____ of the solutions fully addressed the problem.
writingSC.explanation_th: พูดถึง solutions หลายทางและสื่อว่าไม่มีข้อใดแก้ปัญหาได้ ใช้ none (ไม่มีเลย) ไม่ใช้ both หรือ all ที่สื่อว่ามีบางอย่างใช้ได้

writingSC.stem: ____ students who attended the seminar received a certificate.
writingSC.explanation_th: students เป็นพหูพจน์ จึงใช้ all + plural noun เพื่อสื่อว่า 'นักเรียนทุกคน' ในภาพรวม ไม่ใช้ every ที่ตามด้วยเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: Some students finished early, while ____ continued working until the last minute.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตามหลัง while ต้องการประธานพหูพจน์ไม่ตามด้วยนาม ใช้ others แทน 'คนอื่นๆ' ไม่ใช้ other เดี่ยวๆ เพราะต้องมีนามตาม

writingSC.stem: We have two printers in the office. One is broken, but ____ is working properly.
writingSC.explanation_th: มีจำนวนจำกัดชัดเจนสองเครื่อง ใช้ the other เมื่อต้องการพูดถึง 'อีกเครื่องหนึ่งที่เหลือ' ในกลุ่มที่รู้จำนวน

writingSC.stem: This research focuses on two factors. One is economic, and ____ is social.
writingSC.explanation_th: มีสองปัจจัยชัดเจน หนึ่งคือ economic อีกหนึ่งคือ social จึงใช้ the other แทน 'อีกปัจจัยหนึ่ง' ที่เหลืออยู่

writingSC.stem: If this method does not work, we will have to try ____ one.
writingSC.explanation_th: another + singular noun หมายถึง 'อีกอันหนึ่ง' โดยไม่ได้จำกัดจำนวนทั้งหมดของตัวเลือก จึงใช้ another one

writingSC.stem: Some students prefer group work, while ____ prefer to study alone.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตามหลัง while ต้องการประธานพหูพจน์ ใช้ others หมายถึง 'นักเรียนคนอื่นๆ' ที่เหลือ ไม่ใช้ other เดี่ยวๆ ซึ่งต้องมีนามตาม

writingSC.stem: The university requires that ____ of the applicants submit two letters of recommendation.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง ___ of the + พหูพจน์ ใช้ each of ได้ (เน้นทีละคน + กริยาเอกพจน์) ส่วน every of/all of/either of ไม่ถูก: every of ใช้ไม่ได้, either of จำกัดแค่สองคน, all of สื่อรวมกลุ่มไม่ตรง require ทีละราย

writingSC.stem: At the end of the term, ____ of the students received feedback on their performance.
writingSC.explanation_th: students เป็นพหูพจน์ ใช้ all + plural noun เพื่อบอกว่านักเรียนทุกคนโดยรวมได้รับคำแนะนำ ไม่ใช้ every ที่ตามด้วยเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: There are three options available, but ____ of them seems completely satisfactory.
writingSC.explanation_th: มีสามตัวเลือกขึ้นไป ใช้ none (ไม่ตัวใดเลย) ต่างจาก neither/either ที่ใช้กับเพียงสองตัวเลือกเท่านั้น

writingSC.stem: The researchers collected data from two groups, and ____ group was analyzed separately.
writingSC.explanation_th: กล่าวถึงสองกลุ่มที่ถูกวิเคราะห์ทีละกลุ่ม ใช้ each group เน้นทีละหน่วย ส่วน both group ไม่ถูกต้องต้องเป็น both groups

writingSC.stem: The professor met ____ student individually to discuss their research topics.
writingSC.explanation_th: met each student individually แสดงว่าเจอทีละคน ใช้ each + singular noun เพื่อเน้นการกระจายไปยังแต่ละคน

writingSC.stem: Some participants completed the questionnaire online, while ____ filled it out on paper.
writingSC.explanation_th: ต้องการสรรพนามพหูพจน์ทำหน้าที่ประธาน ใช้ others = ผู้เข้าร่วมคนอื่นๆ ที่เหลือ ไม่ใช้ other เดี่ยวๆ เพราะต้องมีนามตาม

writingSC.stem: One of the theories is widely accepted, but ____ are still highly controversial.
writingSC.explanation_th: กล่าวถึงทฤษฎีหลายข้อ ใช้ one ... others เพื่อบอกว่า 'ข้อหนึ่ง' และ 'ข้ออื่นๆ' ดังนั้นใช้ others ในฐานะคำนามไม่ต้องมีนามตาม

writingSC.stem: This experiment differs from ____ previous studies that focused only on short-term effects.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตามหลัง from ต้องมี determiners + นามพหูพจน์ ใช้ other + plural noun → other previous studies ไม่ใช้ others เพราะไม่มีนามตาม

writingSC.stem: The survey allowed respondents to select one topic and suggest ____ if necessary.
writingSC.explanation_th: suggest another หมายถึงเสนอหัวข้อ 'อีกอันหนึ่ง' โดยไม่ระบุชัดเจน ใช้ another เป็นคำนามเดี่ยว ไม่ต้องมีนามตามในบริบทซ้ำ

writingSC.stem: The committee considered one proposal carefully and briefly reviewed ____ before voting.
writingSC.explanation_th: มีชุดข้อเสนอที่กำหนดไว้ชัด ใช้ the others แทน 'ข้อเสนออื่นๆ ที่เหลือทั้งหมด' ต่างจาก others ที่เน้นแบบไม่เฉพาะเจาะจง

writingSC.stem: ____ of the two solutions is ideal, but we have to choose the less problematic one.
writingSC.explanation_th: of the two แสดงว่ามีสองตัวเลือก และบริบทบอกว่าไม่มีตัวไหนสมบูรณ์ ใช้ neither (ไม่ตัวใดเลย) ไม่ใช่ either

writingSC.stem: ____ of the three proposals takes environmental concerns seriously enough.
writingSC.explanation_th: มีสามข้อเสนอขึ้นไป ใช้ none of + plural noun/代noun เพื่อสื่อว่า 'ไม่มีข้อใดเลย' ส่วน neither/either ใช้กับสองตัวเลือกเท่านั้น

writingSC.stem: The university guarantees that ____ student has access to academic advising services.
writingSC.explanation_th: every + singular noun ใช้พูดถึงกลุ่มใหญ่ทั้งหมดแบบทั่วไป เช่น every student ในเชิงนโยบาย ส่วน each เน้นรายคนทีละคนมากกว่า

writingSC.stem: Before the exam, the teacher reminded ____ of the students to check their student ID cards.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง each of + plural noun/代noun ใช้ได้ เช่น each of the students เน้นย้ำไปยังแต่ละคนทีละคน ไม่ใช้ every of ซึ่งผิดรูป

writingSC.stem: ____ the students in this program are required to complete a research project in their final year.
writingSC.explanation_th: all of + plural noun ใช้เน้น 'นักศึกษาทุกคนในโปรแกรมนี้' ในภาพรวม ส่วน every of เป็นรูปที่ใช้ไม่ได้ในไวยากรณ์มาตรฐาน

writingSC.stem: ____ of the solutions proposed in the meeting addresses the root cause of the problem.
writingSC.explanation_th: solutions มีหลายวิธีเกินสอง ใช้ none (ไม่มีวิธีใดเลย) ส่วน either/neither ใช้กับตัวเลือกเพียงสองตัวเท่านั้น

writingSC.stem: After reviewing the data, the researcher found that ____ of the two variables had a significant effect.
writingSC.explanation_th: of the two variables แสดงถึงสองตัวแปร และผลคือไม่มีตัวใดมีผลอย่างมีนัยสำคัญ ใช้ neither (ไม่ตัวใดเลย)

writingSC.stem: The instructor emphasized that ____ student must submit the assignment independently, without copying.
writingSC.explanation_th: each + singular noun เน้นแต่ละคนทีละคน เช่น each student ต้องส่งงานเอง ต่างจาก all student(s) ที่เน้นกลุ่มรวม

writingSC.stem: Because of limited funding, the department can support ____ of the two projects this year, but not both.
writingSC.explanation_th: of the two projects ใช้ either เพื่อสื่อว่า 'โครงการใดโครงการหนึ่ง' สามารถได้รับทุน แต่ไม่สามารถสนับสนุนทั้งคู่

writingSC.stem: The survey revealed that ____ of the participants had read the instructions carefully before answering.
writingSC.explanation_th: participants เป็นพหูพจน์ ใช้ all + plural noun เพื่อสื่อว่าผู้ตอบแบบสอบถามทุกคนอ่านคำชี้แจงเรียบร้อยแล้ว

writingSC.stem: ____ of the two textbooks covers grammar more thoroughly, but the other is better for vocabulary.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง each of the two textbooks เน้นว่าหนังสือทั้งสองเล่มมีจุดเด่นต่างกันทีละเล่ม เหมาะใช้ each of แบ่งคุณสมบัติ

writingSC.stem: The committee announced ____ decision after the meeting.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ its เพราะ committee เป็น collective noun ที่มองเป็นหน่วยเดียว จึงใช้สรรพนามเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: The faculty has submitted ____ proposal to the university board.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ its เพราะ faculty ในโจทย์นี้เป็นคำนามเอกพจน์แบบกลุ่ม จึงใช้ its

writingSC.stem: The statistics indicate that the number of applicants is higher this year, but ____ are still incomplete.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ they เพราะ statistics ในบริบทนี้หมายถึงข้อมูลหลายรายการ จึงอ้างกลับเป็นพหูพจน์

writingSC.stem: The new gym equipment is expensive, and ____ needs regular maintenance.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ it เพราะ equipment เป็นคำนามนับไม่ได้และเอกพจน์ตามรูป

writingSC.stem: These laptops are better than ____ on the shelf.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ those เพราะแทนคำนามพหูพจน์ laptops และเปรียบเทียบกับของที่อยู่บนชั้น

writingSC.stem: The climate of Chiang Mai is cooler than ____ of Bangkok.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ that เพราะใช้แทน noun phrase เอกพจน์ climate เพื่อหลีกเลี่ยงการซ้ำคำ

writingSC.stem: There were two essays. I liked the first one, but not the ____.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ other เพราะมีสองชิ้นและพูดถึงอีกชิ้นที่เหลืออยู่

writingSC.stem: The red shirts are too small. Do you have any ____ in medium size?
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ ones เพราะแทน shirts ซึ่งเป็นพหูพจน์

writingSC.stem: Each of the players must bring ____ own water bottle.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ his or her เพราะ each เป็นเอกพจน์ ทำให้ pronoun ที่อ้างกลับต้องเป็นเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: The news from the exam office ____ surprising to the students.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ was เพราะ news แม้ลงท้าย s แต่เป็นคำนามเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: Neither the teacher nor the students forgot ____ homework.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ their เพราะเมื่อมี neither...nor และ antecedent ใกล้สุดเป็น students ซึ่งเป็นพหูพจน์

writingSC.stem: The scissors are missing. Has anyone seen ____?
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ them เพราะ scissors เป็นคำนามรูปพหูพจน์

writingSC.stem: The information in these tables is useful, but ____ must be checked carefully.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ it เพราะ information เป็นคำนามนับไม่ได้และถือเป็นเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: The two solutions were both acceptable, but the second ____ more practical.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ was เพราะ second one เป็นเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: The shoes in the window are stylish, but the ones inside are even better than ____.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ those เพราะเปรียบเทียบรองเท้าหลายคู่ และ those ใช้แทนพหูพจน์

writingSC.stem: A number of students have already registered, and ____ are waiting outside the office.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ they เพราะ a number of + plural noun ทำให้ความหมายเป็นพหูพจน์

writingSC.stem: The style of the Thai essay is different from ____ of the English essay.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ that เพราะแทน style ซึ่งเป็นเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: The data were collected by the researchers, and ____ will be published next month.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ they เพราะ data ในรูปวิชาการมักใช้เป็นพหูพจน์เมื่อหมายถึงข้อมูลหลายชุด

writingSC.stem: The committee members each submitted ____ own feedback form.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ their เพราะ committee members เป็นพหูพจน์ และ each ยังชี้ไปที่แต่ละคน

writingSC.stem: The old textbook is cheaper than the new ____.
writingSC.explanation_th: คำตอบคือ one เพราะ textbook เป็นนับได้เอกพจน์ จึงใช้ one แทนคำนาม

writingSC.stem: It is essential that every applicant ____ the correct email address.
writingSC.explanation_th: essential that + S + V1 ใช้ provide แม้ประธานเอกพจน์

writingSC.stem: The committee demanded that the proposal ____ in full detail.
writingSC.explanation_th: demanded that + passive subjunctive = be explained

writingSC.stem: It is vital that the university ____ stricter academic standards.
writingSC.explanation_th: after vital that ใช้ bare infinitive adopt

writingSC.stem: The professor insisted that the references ____ accurate and complete.
writingSC.explanation_th: insist that + S + be + adjective เป็น subjunctive

writingSC.stem: It is crucial that the data ____ before publication.
writingSC.explanation_th: crucial that + passive subjunctive = be checked

writingSC.stem: It is important that the report ____ clearly and concisely.
writingSC.explanation_th: important that + passive form ใช้ be written

writingSC.stem: The board required that all employees ____ the new policy.
writingSC.explanation_th: require that + V1 ใช้ follow

writingSC.stem: It is advisable that he ____ his application early.
writingSC.explanation_th: advisable that + subjunctive ใช้ submit

writingSC.stem: She would rather he ____ the issue with the dean.
writingSC.explanation_th: would rather + past simple ใช้ discussed

writingSC.stem: It is necessary that the lab ____ every afternoon.
writingSC.explanation_th: necessary that + passive subjunctive = be cleaned

writingSC.stem: The editor suggested that the article ____ one more example.
writingSC.explanation_th: suggested that + V1 = include

writingSC.stem: It is high time we ____ home.
writingSC.explanation_th: it is high time + past simple ใช้ went

writingSC.stem: If only I ____ more time to revise my essay.
writingSC.explanation_th: If only + past simple ใช้ had เพื่อความปรารถนาในปัจจุบัน

writingSC.stem: The committee asked that all questions ____ in English.
writingSC.explanation_th: ask that + passive subjunctive = be answered

writingSC.stem: I would rather she ____ to the office alone.
writingSC.explanation_th: would rather + past simple ใช้ did not go สำหรับสถานการณ์ปัจจุบัน/อนาคต

writingSC.stem: It is imperative that the scholarship form ____ before the deadline.
writingSC.explanation_th: imperative that + passive subjunctive = be submitted

writingSC.stem: The university recommended that students ____ their IDs at all times.
writingSC.explanation_th: recommended that + V1 = carry

writingSC.stem: It is crucial that the lecture ____ on time.
writingSC.explanation_th: crucial that + bare infinitive start

writingSC.stem: The dean insisted that the exam room ____ silent.
writingSC.explanation_th: insist that + V1 = remain

writingSC.stem: It is high time the company ____ its outdated software.
writingSC.explanation_th: it is high time + past simple ใช้ replaced

writingSC.stem: It is vital that all participants ____ the instructions carefully.
writingSC.explanation_th: vital that + V1 = read

writingSC.stem: The dean required that the students ____ for identification.
writingSC.explanation_th: require that + V1 = show

writingSC.stem: It is essential that the evidence ____ to the court.
writingSC.explanation_th: essential that + passive subjunctive = be presented

writingSC.stem: The board insisted that the budget ____ reviewed again.
writingSC.explanation_th: insist that + be + participle

writingSC.stem: It is crucial that she ____ all the references accurately.
writingSC.explanation_th: crucial that + V1 = cite

writingSC.stem: He would rather the meeting ____ after lunch.
writingSC.explanation_th: would rather + past simple = started

writingSC.stem: It is high time the company ____ its employees better.
