writingSC.explanation_th: it is high time + past simple = treated

writingSC.stem: If only the document ____ before submission.
writingSC.explanation_th: If only + past perfect ใช้ had been checked

writingSC.stem: The committee proposed that the survey ____ online.
writingSC.explanation_th: proposed that + passive subjunctive = be conducted

writingSC.stem: It is important that every answer ____ clearly.
writingSC.explanation_th: important that + passive subjunctive = be written

writingSC.stem: She would rather he ____ the matter until tomorrow.
writingSC.explanation_th: would rather + past simple = delayed

writingSC.stem: It is necessary that the lights ____ after class.
writingSC.explanation_th: necessary that + passive subjunctive = be turned off

writingSC.stem: The professor suggested that every student ____ one question.
writingSC.explanation_th: suggested that + V1 = ask

writingSC.stem: It is imperative that the file ____ securely.
writingSC.explanation_th: imperative that + passive subjunctive = be stored

writingSC.stem: It is high time we ____ a decision.
writingSC.explanation_th: it is high time + past simple = made

writingSC.stem: The director insisted that the staff ____ formal clothing.
writingSC.explanation_th: insist that + V1 = wear

writingSC.stem: The teacher made the students ____ the essay again.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb make ใน Active Voice ต้องตามด้วย Bare Infinitive (V1 ไม่มี to)

writingSC.stem: I heard someone ____ my name from the next room.
writingSC.explanation_th: hear + Object + Bare Infinitive แสดงว่าได้ยินทั้งกระบวนการ

writingSC.stem: ____ hard work is essential for success.
writingSC.explanation_th: Gerund (V-ing) ทำหน้าที่เป็น Subject ของประโยค

writingSC.stem: Let him ____ the decision himself.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb let ต้องตามด้วย Bare Infinitive (V1 ไม่มี to) เสมอ

writingSC.stem: We saw the birds ____ across the sky.
writingSC.explanation_th: see + Object + Bare Infinitive แสดงว่าเห็นการกระทำทั้งหมดจบเลย

writingSC.stem: ____ in the library is prohibited during exam hours.
writingSC.explanation_th: Gerund (V-ing) ทำหน้าที่เป็น Subject ของประโยค ใช้ประกาศกฎกติกา

writingSC.stem: Have the mechanic ____ your car before the long trip.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb have + Object + Bare Infinitive (ให้ใครทำอะไร)

writingSC.stem: I watched the children ____ in the park yesterday.
writingSC.explanation_th: watch + Object + Bare Infinitive แสดงว่าเห็นการกระทำทั้งหมด

writingSC.stem: ____ mathematics requires logical thinking.
writingSC.explanation_th: Gerund (V-ing) ทำหน้าที่เป็น Subject ของประโยค

writingSC.stem: The boss had his assistant ____ the report immediately.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb have + Object + Bare Infinitive (สั่งให้ใครทำอะไร)

writingSC.stem: I noticed him ____ out of the window during the meeting.
writingSC.explanation_th: notice + Object + Bare Infinitive แสดงว่าเห็นการกระทำทั้งหมด

writingSC.stem: ____ regular exercise is important for health.
writingSC.explanation_th: Gerund (V-ing) ทำหน้าที่เป็น Subject ของประโยค

writingSC.stem: Let us ____ the matter seriously.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb let ต้องตามด้วย Bare Infinitive (V1 ไม่มี to)

writingSC.stem: She heard her name ____ in the conversation.
writingSC.explanation_th: Passive ของ Verb of perception: hear + Object + V3 (ถูกพูดถึง)

writingSC.stem: ____ five languages makes him very employable.
writingSC.explanation_th: Gerund (V-ing) ทำหน้าที่เป็น Subject ของประโยค

writingSC.stem: The teacher let the students ____ early today.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb let ต้องตามด้วย Bare Infinitive (V1 ไม่มี to)

writingSC.stem: I saw the thief ____ into the building last night.
writingSC.explanation_th: see + Object + Bare Infinitive แสดงว่าเห็นการกระทำทั้งหมด

writingSC.stem: ____ properly is essential for good health.
writingSC.explanation_th: Gerund (V-ing) ทำหน้าที่เป็น Subject ของประโยค

writingSC.stem: Have your brother ____ you with the moving.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb have + Object + Bare Infinitive (ให้ใครทำอะไร)

writingSC.stem: We watched the sun ____ behind the mountains.
writingSC.explanation_th: watch + Object + Bare Infinitive แสดงว่าเห็นการกระทำทั้งหมด

writingSC.stem: ____ in the team requires good communication skills.
writingSC.explanation_th: Gerund (V-ing) ทำหน้าที่เป็น Subject ของประโยค

writingSC.stem: Let them ____ the event themselves.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb let ต้องตามด้วย Bare Infinitive (V1 ไม่มี to)

writingSC.stem: I heard the music ____ loudly from the next room.
writingSC.explanation_th: hear + Object + Bare Infinitive แสดงว่าได้ยินการกระทำทั้งหมด

writingSC.stem: ____ a foreign language opens many opportunities.
writingSC.explanation_th: Gerund (V-ing) ทำหน้าที่เป็น Subject ของประโยค

writingSC.stem: The manager had his secretary ____ the letters.
writingSC.explanation_th: Causative verb have + Object + Bare Infinitive (สั่งให้ใครทำอะไร)

writingSC.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: The professor explained ______ was expected in the final report.
writingSC.explanation_th: เป็น noun clause ทำหน้าที่เป็น object ของ explained โครงสร้างคือ Main Clause + Dependent Noun Clause

writingSC.stem: Choose the structurally correct sentence.
options: The article that was published yesterday received attention.
The article published yesterday, received attention.
The article that published yesterday received attention.
The article, that was published yesterday received attention.
writingSC.explanation_th: that was published yesterday เป็น restrictive relative clause ขยาย article ได้ถูกต้อง

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct punctuation: The data were incomplete ______ the researcher repeated the survey.
writingSC.explanation_th: สอง Main Clause ต้องใช้ semicolon ก่อน conjunctive adverb however และตามด้วย comma

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: Although the experiment was simple, the results were surprising.
Although the experiment was simple the results were surprising.
The experiment was simple although, the results were surprising.
Although was simple the experiment, the results were surprising.
writingSC.explanation_th: Dependent Adverb Clause Although the experiment was simple นำหน้า Main Clause ต้องมี comma คั่น

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct noun clause: The committee discussed ______ should be included in the new policy.
writingSC.explanation_th: what should be included เป็น noun clause ทำหน้าที่เป็น object ของ discussed

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct clause structure.
options: The team worked hard, therefore, they finished early.
The team worked hard; therefore, they finished early.
The team worked hard therefore they finished early.
The team worked hard, therefore they finished early.
writingSC.explanation_th: therefore เป็น conjunctive adverb ระหว่างสอง Main Clause จึงใช้ semicolon ก่อนและ comma หลัง

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: Because the researcher revised the draft, it became clearer.
Because the researcher revised the draft it became clearer.
The draft became clearer because, the researcher revised it.
The draft became clearer because revised the researcher it.
writingSC.explanation_th: Adverb Clause of cause Because... นำหน้า Main Clause ต้องมี comma คั่น

writingSC.stem: Choose the option that correctly uses a relative clause.
options: Students who submitted the assignment on time received feedback.
Students, who submitted the assignment on time received feedback.
Students who submitted the assignment on time, received feedback.
Students submitted the assignment on time who received feedback.
writingSC.explanation_th: Relative clause who submitted the assignment on time ขยายคำนาม Students ได้ถูกต้อง

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct noun clause: The issue is ______ the policy should be changed immediately.
writingSC.explanation_th: whether the policy should be changed immediately เป็น noun clause complement หลัง is

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: The researcher who discovered the error was praised.
The researcher, who discovered the error was praised.
The researcher who discovered the error, was praised.
The researcher who, discovered the error was praised.
writingSC.explanation_th: Relative clause who discovered the error ขยาย researcher และ Main Clause สมบูรณ์

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct punctuation: The study was expensive ______ it was highly valuable.
writingSC.explanation_th: however เป็น conjunctive adverb ต้องใช้ semicolon ก่อนและ comma หลัง

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence that correctly uses an adverb clause of condition.
options: If the weather improves, the event will continue.
If the weather improves the event will continue.
The event will continue if, the weather improves.
If improves the weather, the event will continue.
writingSC.explanation_th: Dependent Clause of condition If... นำหน้า Main Clause ต้องมี comma

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: The report, which was submitted yesterday, contains several errors.
The report which was submitted yesterday contains, several errors.
The report which was submitted yesterday, contains several errors.
The report, which was submitted yesterday contains several errors.
writingSC.explanation_th: Non-restrictive relative clause ต้องมี comma คั่นทั้งสองด้าน

writingSC.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: The lecturer suggested ______ the assignment be revised.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause หลัง suggested ใช้โครงสร้าง formal suggested that + subject + base verb

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: The experiment failed because the equipment was defective.
Because the experiment failed the equipment was defective.
The experiment failed, because, the equipment was defective.
The experiment failed because, the equipment was defective.
writingSC.explanation_th: Main Clause ตามด้วย adverb clause of cause because... ถูกต้อง

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct clause coordination.
options: The results were promising, therefore the team expanded the study.
The results were promising; therefore, the team expanded the study.
The results were promising therefore, the team expanded the study.
The results were promising, therefore, the team expanded the study.
writingSC.explanation_th: therefore เป็น conjunctive adverb ต้องใช้ semicolon ก่อนและ comma หลัง

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct noun clause: The problem is ______ no one reported the error earlier.
writingSC.explanation_th: that no one reported the error earlier เป็น noun clause ทำหน้าที่ complement หลัง is

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: The author whose book was translated into Thai visited the university.
The author, whose book was translated into Thai visited the university.
The author whose book was translated into Thai, visited the university.
The author whose, book was translated into Thai visited the university.
writingSC.explanation_th: Relative clause whose book... ขยาย author ถูกต้อง

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct punctuation: The results were inconsistent ______ the researcher repeated the analysis.
writingSC.explanation_th: therefore เป็น conjunctive adverb ต้องใช้ semicolon ก่อนและ comma หลัง

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct adverb clause usage.
options: Since the evidence was weak, the court dismissed the claim.
Since the evidence was weak the court dismissed the claim, however.
The court dismissed the claim since, the evidence was weak.
Since weak was the evidence, the court dismissed the claim.
writingSC.explanation_th: Dependent Clause of reason Since... นำหน้า Main Clause ต้องใช้ comma

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: The plan that the committee approved will start next month.
The plan, that the committee approved, will start next month.
The plan that the committee approved, will start next month.
The plan which the committee approved will start, next month.
writingSC.explanation_th: Restrictive relative clause that... จำเป็นต่อความหมายและไม่ใช้ comma

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct completion: The teacher insisted ______ the students submit their essays on time.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause หลัง insisted ใช้โครงสร้าง formal insisted that + subject + base verb

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct coordination.
options: The sample was contaminated, but the team continued the test.
The sample was contaminated but, the team continued the test.
The sample was contaminated, the team continued the test.
The sample was contaminated because the team continued the test.
writingSC.explanation_th: สอง Main Clause เชื่อมด้วย comma + coordinating conjunction but ถูกต้อง

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: Although the weather was bad, the flight departed on time.
Although the weather was bad the flight departed on time, nevertheless.
The flight departed on time although, the weather was bad.
Although was bad the weather, the flight departed on time.
writingSC.explanation_th: Dependent concessive clause Although... นำหน้า Main Clause ต้องคั่นด้วย comma

writingSC.stem: Choose the structurally correct sentence.
options: The study was improved, furthermore the methodology was clearer.
The study was improved; furthermore, the methodology was clearer.
The study was improved furthermore, the methodology was clearer.
The study was improved, furthermore, the methodology was clearer.
writingSC.explanation_th: furthermore เป็น conjunctive adverb จึงต้องใช้ semicolon ก่อนและ comma หลัง

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct noun clause: The concern is ______ the funding will arrive in time.
writingSC.explanation_th: whether the funding will arrive in time เป็น noun clause complement หลัง linking verb

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: The paper that was accepted yesterday will appear next month.
The paper, that was accepted yesterday, will appear next month.
The paper that was accepted yesterday, will appear next month.
The paper which was accepted yesterday will appear, next month.
writingSC.explanation_th: Restrictive relative clause that... ระบุ paper ให้ชัดและ Main Clause สมบูรณ์

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct punctuation: The data were limited ______ the conclusion remained tentative.
writingSC.explanation_th: however เป็น conjunctive adverb ต้องมี semicolon ก่อนและ comma หลัง

writingSC.stem: Choose the sentence with correct adverb clause usage.
options: When the committee meets, the chair will present the plan.
When the committee meets the chair will present the plan.
The chair will present the plan when, the committee meets.
When the committee meeting, the chair will present the plan.
writingSC.explanation_th: Dependent Adverb Clause of time When... นำหน้า Main Clause จึงใช้ comma

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct sentence.
options: The scientists, who worked overnight, completed the trial.
The scientists who worked overnight completed, the trial.
The scientists who worked overnight, completed the trial.
The scientists, who worked overnight completed the trial.
writingSC.explanation_th: Non-restrictive relative clause ต้องคั่นด้วย comma

writingSC.stem: Choose the correct completion: The board recommended ______ the proposal be postponed.
writingSC.explanation_th: noun clause หลัง recommended ใช้โครงสร้าง formal recommended that + subject + base verb

writingSC.stem: By the time we arrived at the cinema, the film __________ already.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — by the time + อดีต → Past Perfect: had started

writingSC.stem: She __________ in this company for over a decade and shows no sign of leaving.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — for + ช่วงเวลาถึงปัจจุบัน → Present Perfect: has worked

writingSC.stem: By next June, I __________ all the required courses for my degree.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — By + เวลาในอนาคต (เสร็จก่อนถึงตอนนั้น) → Future Perfect

writingSC.stem: While the committee __________ the budget, the power suddenly went out.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — เหตุการณ์กำลังดำเนินในอดีตแล้วถูกขัดจังหวะ → Past Continuous

writingSC.stem: As soon as the results __________, the school will inform every candidate.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — อนุประโยคเวลา (as soon as) ใช้ปัจจุบันแทนอนาคต → are announced

writingSC.stem: Each of the students __________ required to submit a report.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — each of + พหูพจน์ → กริยาเอกพจน์ is

writingSC.stem: Neither the teacher nor her students __________ aware of the change.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — neither...nor ผันตามคำใกล้สุด (students) → were

writingSC.stem: The number of applicants __________ increased sharply this year.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — The number of... เป็นเอกพจน์ → has increased

writingSC.stem: Mathematics __________ my favourite subject when I was at school.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — Mathematics (ชื่อวิชา) เป็นเอกพจน์ → was

writingSC.stem: __________ the heavy rain, the match continued as planned.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — Despite + นามวลี (the heavy rain) → Despite

writingSC.stem: He studied very hard; __________, he passed the difficult exam.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ความสัมพันธ์เหตุ→ผล → therefore

writingSC.stem: __________ she was extremely tired, she finished the project that night.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ตามด้วยประโยค + ความขัดแย้ง → Although she was tired

writingSC.stem: The plan failed __________ a serious lack of funding.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ตามด้วยนามวลี → because of a lack of funding

writingSC.stem: We can either take the train __________ drive to the coast.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — either ... or

writingSC.stem: The scientist __________ discovery changed medicine received a major award.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — แสดงความเป็นเจ้าของ (discovery ของนักวิทยาศาสตร์) → whose

writingSC.stem: This is the house __________ my grandparents lived for fifty years.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ขยายสถานที่ (lived in a place) → where

writingSC.stem: The students __________ submitted their work late received a lower grade.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — อ้างถึงคน (students) เป็นประธาน → who

writingSC.stem: The novel, __________ was published in 1925, is still widely read today.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — อนุประโยคไม่จำกัดความ (มี ,) ใช้ which ไม่ใช่ that

writingSC.stem: If I __________ about the meeting, I would have attended it.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — เงื่อนไขอดีต (would have attended) → If + had known (3rd)

writingSC.stem: If the weather __________ fine tomorrow, we will go hiking.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — เงื่อนไขที่ 1: If + Present → is

writingSC.stem: If she studied harder, she __________ much better grades.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — เงื่อนไขที่ 2 (studied) → would get

writingSC.stem: __________ I in your position, I would accept the offer at once.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — Inversion ของเงื่อนไขที่ 2: Were I (= If I were)

writingSC.stem: She avoided __________ eye contact during the difficult interview.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — avoid + V-ing → avoid making

writingSC.stem: They finally decided __________ the project despite the difficulties.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — decide + to-infinitive → decided to continue

writingSC.stem: I look forward to __________ from you in the near future.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — look forward to + V-ing → hearing

writingSC.stem: He is mainly responsible for __________ the new employees.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — responsible for + V-ing → training

writingSC.stem: The job requires speaking clearly, writing accurately, and __________ well with others.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ขนานกับ speaking, writing → working

writingSC.stem: She is admired for her intelligence, her kindness, and __________.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ขนานกับ her intelligence, her kindness → her honesty

writingSC.stem: We spent the whole afternoon swimming, sunbathing, and __________ sandcastles.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ขนานกับ swimming, sunbathing → building

writingSC.stem: Never before __________ such a magnificent performance.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — Never before ขึ้นต้น → ผันกริยาช่วยมาก่อนประธาน: have I seen

writingSC.stem: Not only __________ the exam, but she also won a scholarship.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — Not only ขึ้นต้น → did she pass

writingSC.stem: Hardly __________ the room when the telephone rang.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — Hardly ... when → Hardly had I entered the room when...

writingSC.stem: This year's results are far better than __________.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — เปรียบเทียบ results (พหูพจน์) → those of last year

writingSC.stem: The more you practise, __________ you will become.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — โครงสร้าง the more..., the more... → the more confident

writingSC.stem: She is __________ student in the entire class.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ขั้นสูงสุดในกลุ่ม → the most hardworking

writingSC.stem: The success of the event depends __________ careful planning.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — depend on

writingSC.stem: She has always been deeply interested __________ ancient history.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — interested in

writingSC.stem: The committee consists __________ ten experienced members.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — consist of

writingSC.stem: He apologised __________ arriving so late to the meeting.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — apologise for + V-ing → for arriving

writingSC.stem: There isn't __________ information available about the new policy.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — information นับไม่ได้ → much

writingSC.stem: She is __________ honest person I have ever met.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ขั้นสูงสุด → the most honest person

writingSC.stem: Only __________ students passed the extremely difficult test.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — students นับได้ → a few

writingSC.stem: You __________ submit the form by Friday, or your application will be rejected.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ความจำเป็น/บังคับ → must submit

writingSC.stem: The doctor recommended that the patient __________ more rest.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — recommend that + ประธาน + กริยาช่อง 1 (subjunctive) → get

writingSC.stem: When I was young, I __________ spend hours reading comic books.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — นิสัยในอดีต → would spend (= used to spend)

writingSC.stem: It is essential that every member __________ present at the meeting.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — It is essential that + ประธาน + (should) be → be present

writingSC.stem: She asked me __________ I had finished the report yet.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ถามแบบ yes/no ทางอ้อม → whether (หรือ if)

writingSC.stem: The teacher told us that the test __________ postponed until the following week.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — reported speech (told...อดีต) → had been postponed

writingSC.stem: Could you please tell me __________ the nearest bus stop is?
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — คำถามฝัง เรียงประธาน+กริยา → where the nearest bus stop is

writingSC.stem: Despite __________ very hard, he failed to finish the marathon.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — Despite + V-ing → Despite trying very hard

writingSC.stem: She had her hair __________ before the wedding ceremony.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — have + กรรม + V3 (causative) → had her hair cut

writingSC.stem: We need to get the air conditioner __________ before summer arrives.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — get + กรรม + V3 → get the air conditioner repaired

writingSC.stem: The manager had his assistant __________ the report by noon.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — have + คน + กริยาช่อง 1 (active causative) → had his assistant finish

writingSC.stem: I wish I __________ harder for the final exam last term.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — wish + เสียดายอดีต → Past Perfect: had studied

writingSC.stem: She wishes she __________ taller so she could join the basketball team.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — wish (ปัจจุบันตรงข้ามความจริง) → were taller

writingSC.stem: If only I __________ his advice, I wouldn't be in trouble now.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — If only + เสียดายอดีต → had taken

writingSC.stem: The ancient temple __________ by thousands of tourists every year.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — วัดถูกเยี่ยมชม (Present Passive) → is visited

writingSC.stem: A new bridge __________ across the river at the moment.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — กำลังถูกสร้าง (Present Continuous Passive) → is being built

writingSC.stem: The results __________ by the end of next week.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ผลจะถูกประกาศ (Future Passive) → will be announced

writingSC.stem: She isn't answering her phone; she __________ already left the office.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — สรุปอดีตอย่างมั่นใจ → must have left

writingSC.stem: You __________ told me earlier; I would have helped you.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ตำหนิอดีตที่ไม่ได้ทำ → should have told me

writingSC.stem: The keys aren't here, so I __________ left them at home.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — คาดเดาอดีตอย่างไม่แน่ใจ → might have left

writingSC.stem: She spoke very slowly __________ everyone could understand her.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — จุดประสงค์ + ประโยค (subject + could) → so that everyone could understand

writingSC.stem: He left home early __________ avoid the heavy morning traffic.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — จุดประสงค์ + กริยา (avoid) → in order to avoid

writingSC.stem: They saved money for years __________ they could buy a house.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — จุดประสงค์ + ประโยค → so that they could buy

writingSC.stem: Could you please __________ me a favour and close the window?
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — do somebody a favour → do me a favour

writingSC.stem: We need to __________ a decision about the venue today.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — make a decision

writingSC.stem: She decided to __________ a break after working for six hours.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — take a break

writingSC.stem: They will __________ a meeting tomorrow to discuss the budget.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — have a meeting

writingSC.stem: The cat is hiding __________ the sofa, where no one can see it.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ใต้โซฟา → under the sofa

writingSC.stem: She walked __________ the room and sat down at the front.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — การเคลื่อนที่เข้าไป → walked into the room

writingSC.stem: The painting hangs __________ the wall above the fireplace.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ติดบนผนัง → on the wall

writingSC.stem: Please __________ your shoes before entering the house.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ถอด (รองเท้า) → take off your shoes

writingSC.stem: I need to __________ this difficult word in the dictionary.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ค้นหาความหมาย → look up the word

writingSC.stem: The meeting was __________ until next Monday because of the storm.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — เลื่อนออกไป → put off (postpone)

writingSC.stem: __________ of the twins has a completely different personality.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — เน้นทีละคนจากสอง → Each of the twins (+ กริยาเอกพจน์)

writingSC.stem: I have read __________ the books on the recommended list.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — all + the + นามพหูพจน์ → all the books

writingSC.stem: __________ student must bring an identity card to the exam.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — Every + นามเอกพจน์ → Every student

writingSC.stem: This summer is not __________ last summer.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — โครงสร้าง as + adj + as → as hot as

writingSC.stem: The second test was __________ difficult than the first one.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — less + adj → less difficult than

writingSC.stem: Of all the students in the class, she works __________.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ขั้นสูงสุด + the → the hardest

writingSC.stem: __________ he said at the meeting surprised absolutely everyone.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — นามานุประโยคเป็นประธาน (สิ่งที่เขาพูด) → What he said

writingSC.stem: I really don't know __________ to solve this difficult problem.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — how + to + V → how to solve

writingSC.stem: Nobody is quite sure __________ the meeting will be held.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — สถานที่ → where the meeting will be held

writingSC.stem: The teacher told the students __________ during the examination.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — คำสั่งทางอ้อมเชิงห้าม → told them not to talk

writingSC.stem: She politely asked me __________ the door quietly.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ขอร้องทางอ้อม → asked me to close

writingSC.stem: __________ being very expensive, the restaurant is always fully booked.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — Despite + V-ing → Despite being expensive

writingSC.stem: He is extremely wealthy; __________, he lives a very simple life.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ความขัดแย้ง → nevertheless (อย่างไรก็ตาม)

writingSC.stem: I __________ for three hours by the time the train finally arrived.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ดำเนินต่อเนื่องก่อนอดีต → Past Perfect Continuous: had been waiting

writingSC.stem: This time tomorrow, we __________ over the Pacific Ocean.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — เหตุการณ์กำลังดำเนินในอนาคต (This time tomorrow) → Future Continuous

writingSC.stem: She __________ English since she was a little child.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — since + ต่อเนื่องถึงปัจจุบัน → Present Perfect Continuous

writingSC.stem: My father plays __________ guitar beautifully every evening.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — play + the + เครื่องดนตรี → play the guitar

writingSC.stem: After graduating, she now works as __________ engineer at a large firm.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — อาชีพเอกพจน์ + เสียงสระ → an engineer

writingSC.stem: These shoes are too small; do you have any larger __________?
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — แทนนามพหูพจน์ (shoes) → larger ones

writingSC.stem: I lost my umbrella, so I really need to buy a new __________.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — แทนนามนับได้เอกพจน์ → a new one

writingSC.stem: You won't pass the test __________ you study regularly.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — unless = if...not → unless you study

writingSC.stem: __________ you need any further help, please don't hesitate to call.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — Inversion เงื่อนไข: Should you need help (= If you should need)

writingSC.stem: There __________ a lot of people waiting outside the hall.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — a lot of people (พหูพจน์) → there were

writingSC.stem: Either of these two routes __________ to the station.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — Either of + พหูพจน์ → กริยาเอกพจน์ leads

writingSC.stem: I really don't mind __________ you with your homework tonight.
writingSC.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — mind + V-ing → don't mind helping

writingSC.stem: Not until the stringent new regulations were fully implemented ______ a significant decline in industrial emissions across the region.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง Inversion หลัง Not until ต้องสลับกริยาช่วยไว้หน้าประธาน (did the agency observe)

writingSC.stem: Given the volatile nature of the chemical compound, it is imperative that the storage temperature ______ rigorously monitored at all times.
writingSC.explanation_th: Subjunctive หลัง it is imperative that ต้องใช้ V. infinitive ไม่ผันรูป (be)

writingSC.stem: If the executive board had invested more heavily in renewable energy research a decade ago, the corporation ______ the industry leader today.
writingSC.explanation_th: Mixed conditional: เงื่อนไขสมมติอดีต (had invested) ส่งผลถึงปัจจุบัน (would be) สังเกตคำว่า today

writingSC.stem: The architect ______ the structural flaws during the initial inspection, but the oversight wasn't discovered until the foundation cracked.
writingSC.explanation_th: should have + V3 (ควรทำแต่ไม่ได้ทำ) สอดคล้องกับคำว่า oversight (ความผิดพลาดที่ไม่ถูกพบ)

writingSC.stem: It was the unprecedented combination of severe drought and exceptionally high winds ______ the devastating spread of the wildfires.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง Cleft sentence: It was + X + that + กริยา เน้นสาเหตุ

writingSC.stem: Despite facing numerous setbacks, the research team remained absolutely ______ in their pursuit of a groundbreaking cure for the disease.
writingSC.explanation_th: resolute (แน่วแน่) เข้ากับ pursuit of a cure ในเชิงบวก ส่วน stubborn/inflexible มีนัยเชิงลบ ไม่เข้ากับบริบทชื่นชม

writingSC.stem: ______ the severe financial constraints of the project, the engineering team managed to design a remarkably efficient filtration system.
writingSC.explanation_th: Notwithstanding (แม้ว่า) เป็นบุพบทแสดงการขัดแย้ง ตรงกับใจความ 'แม้มีข้อจำกัด แต่ยังทำสำเร็จ'

writingSC.stem: The professor's argument was so ______ that even his most vocal critics were eventually forced to concede the validity of his main points.
writingSC.explanation_th: compelling (น่าเชื่อถือ/มีน้ำหนัก) สอดคล้องกับการที่นักวิจารณ์ต้องยอมรับ (concede)

writingSC.stem: Hardly ______ the ambitious restructuring plan when a sudden economic downturn forced them to reconsider their entire strategy.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง Hardly had + S + V3 + when ต้องใช้ Past Perfect แบบสลับที่

writingSC.stem: The more meticulously the archaeologists excavated the ancient ruins, ______ they uncovered about the civilization's complex trade networks.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง The more..., the more... ต้องมี the นำหน้า comparative เสมอทั้งสองประโยค

writingSC.stem: Rarely ______ such a meticulously designed algorithm that can accurately predict unpredictable market fluctuations.
writingSC.explanation_th: Inversion หลัง Rarely (negative adverbial) ต้องนำกริยาช่วยมาไว้หน้าประธาน (has the committee)

writingSC.stem: Given the unprecedented severity of the data breach, it is crucial that the chief security officer ______ immediately to prevent further damage.
writingSC.explanation_th: Mandative subjunctive หลัง it is crucial that ต้องใช้ V.พื้นฐาน (be replaced)

writingSC.stem: If the pharmaceutical company had secured the international patent earlier, they ______ the dominant market leader right now.
writingSC.explanation_th: Mixed conditional: เงื่อนไขสมมติอดีต (had secured) ส่งผลถึงปัจจุบัน (would be) สังเกตคำว่า right now

writingSC.stem: The lead architect ______ the structural vulnerabilities during the blueprint phase, but the oversight was completely ignored until the foundation cracked.
writingSC.explanation_th: should have + V3 (ควรทำแต่ไม่ได้ทำ) สอดคล้องกับ oversight ที่ถูกมองข้าม

writingSC.stem: ______ comprehensive empirical data from various ecosystems, the researchers were finally able to publish their groundbreaking findings.
writingSC.explanation_th: Perfect participle (Having + V3) แสดงเหตุการณ์ที่เสร็จก่อนประธานหลัก (the researchers) ทำอีกอย่าง

writingSC.stem: The municipal government implemented stricter zoning laws in a desperate attempt to ______ the negative impacts of rapid urban sprawl.
writingSC.explanation_th: mitigate (บรรเทา/ลดผลกระทบ) เข้ากับบริบทพยายามลด negative impacts

writingSC.stem: ______ the unexpected economic downturn, the board of directors firmly decided to freeze all hiring indefinitely.
writingSC.explanation_th: In light of (เมื่อพิจารณาถึง) ให้ความหมายเชิงเหตุผลที่ถูกต้อง

writingSC.stem: It was not merely his technical expertise but his profound emotional intelligence ______ him an exceptionally effective leader.
writingSC.explanation_th: โครงสร้าง Cleft sentence It was...that + กริยา เน้นย้ำส่วนที่อยู่หน้า that

writingSC.stem: Patients with severely compromised immune systems are highly ______ to opportunistic infections that would normally be harmless.
writingSC.explanation_th: susceptible to (อ่อนแอ/ติดเชื้อง่าย) เข้ากับ compromised immune systems

writingSC.stem: By the time the international summit concludes next week, the delegates ______ a comprehensive and legally binding climate treaty.
writingSC.explanation_th: By the time + ปัจจุบัน(อนาคต) ต้องคู่กับ Future Perfect (will have ratified) แสดงว่าเสร็จก่อนจุดอ้างอิง

readingShort.title: Email: English Camp Sign-Up
readingShort.passage:
From: Ms. Pranee Wong, English Club Coordinator
To: All English Club Members
Subject: English Camp — Important Details and Sign-Up

Dear members,

I am pleased to announce that our annual English Camp will take place on Saturday 15 March at the school hall, from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. This year's theme is "Storytelling Through Drama," and we are lucky to have a guest speaker from Khon Kaen University leading the afternoon workshop.

Please note the following points:
- The camp is free, but places are limited to forty students.
- To reserve a place, reply to this email by Friday 7 March.
- Bring a packed lunch; drinks and snacks will be provided.
- Wear comfortable clothes, as some activities involve movement.

If you have already signed up for the debate competition, you may still join the camp, but please tell me in advance so that I can arrange the schedule.

I look forward to seeing many of you there. Feel free to contact me if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Ms. Pranee Wong
English Club Coordinator

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?
options: To announce an English Camp and explain how to join.
To cancel the school's annual English Camp this year.
To invite members to a debate competition next week.
To report on the results of last year's English Camp.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — อีเมลประกาศการจัด English Camp และบอกวิธีสมัคร (reply by Friday 7 March) ส่วน B/C/D ขัดกับเนื้อหา (ไม่ได้ยกเลิก ไม่ใช่การแข่ง debate และไม่ได้รายงานผลปีก่อน)

questions.stem: How can a student reserve a place at the camp?
options: By paying a small fee at the school office.
By calling the guest speaker before the camp.
By replying to the email before Friday 7 March.
By signing a form at the hall on the camp day.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — อีเมลระบุว่า "To reserve a place, reply to this email by Friday 7 March" ส่วนค่ายนี้ฟรี (ไม่ต้องจ่าย) จึงตัดข้ออื่นออก

questions.stem: What are students asked to bring to the camp?
options: Their own bottles of water and snacks.
A packed lunch for the middle of the day.
A laptop computer for the drama workshop.
A ticket bought from the coordinator first.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — อีเมลบอกว่า "Bring a packed lunch; drinks and snacks will be provided" ดังนั้นต้องเตรียมอาหารกลางวันมาเอง ส่วนเครื่องดื่มและของว่างทางค่ายจัดให้

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the English Camp?
options: It is expected to attract very few students.
It will be held outdoors in the school garden.
It is open only to students who can pay a fee.
It is popular, so places may fill up quickly.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ค่ายฟรีแต่ "places are limited to forty" และให้รีบ reply ภายในวันที่กำหนด จึงอนุมานได้ว่าเป็นที่นิยมและที่นั่งอาจเต็มเร็ว

questions.stem: In the email, the word "reserve" is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — "reserve a place" หมายถึงจองที่ไว้ล่วงหน้า ตรงกับ "to book ahead" ไม่ใช่คืน จ่ายเงิน หรือบรรยาย

questions.stem: How would you best describe the tone of the email?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ผู้เขียนใช้ภาษาสุภาพเป็นกันเอง (Dear members, I look forward to seeing you, Feel free to contact me) พร้อมให้ข้อมูลชัดเจน จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงเป็นมิตรและให้ข้อมูล

readingShort.title: Notice: Library Hours During Exams
readingShort.passage:
NOTICE — School Library

To all students,

During the final examination period (1–14 October), the library will extend its opening hours to help you prepare.

New opening hours:
- Monday to Friday: 7 a.m. – 8 p.m.
- Saturday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m.
- Sunday: closed

Please remember the following rules:
- Keep your voice low. The study zone on the second floor is for silent study only.
- You may borrow up to five books at a time during this period.
- Food is not allowed inside, but covered water bottles are fine.

Students who return books late will not be able to borrow again until the fine has been paid.

We wish you the very best of luck in your exams.

The Library Team

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?
options: To announce that the library will close for exams.
To remind students to return all their library books.
To inform students of extended hours during exams.
To introduce a new study zone on the first floor.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ประกาศแจ้งว่าห้องสมุด "extend its opening hours" ช่วงสอบ ส่วน A ตรงข้ามกับเนื้อหา, B/D เป็นรายละเอียดเล็กน้อยหรือไม่ตรง

questions.stem: When is the library open on Saturdays during this period?
options: From 9 a.m. until 5 p.m. in the afternoon.
From 7 a.m. until 8 p.m. in the evening.
It stays completely closed all of Saturday.
From 9 a.m. until 8 p.m. without any break.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ประกาศระบุ "Saturday: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m." ส่วน 7–20 น. คือจันทร์–ศุกร์ และวันอาทิตย์ปิด จึงตัดข้ออื่นออก

questions.stem: What does the notice say about the second-floor study zone?
options: It is reserved only for teachers during exams.
It is the place where students may eat lunch.
It is closed throughout the whole exam period.
It is for silent study, so voices must be low.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ประกาศบอกว่าโซนชั้นสองเป็น "silent study only" และให้เก็บเสียงเบา ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่มีในประกาศ

questions.stem: What will happen to a student who returns books late?
options: They will be banned from the library forever.
They cannot borrow again until the fine is paid.
They must pay double the price of the books.
They will be reported to their own class teacher.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ประกาศระบุว่าคนคืนหนังสือช้า "will not be able to borrow again until the fine has been paid" ตัวเลือกอื่นเกินจริงหรือไม่มีในบท

questions.stem: In the notice, the word "extend" is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — "extend its hours" หมายถึงขยาย/ทำให้นานขึ้น ตรงกับ "to make longer" ไม่ใช่ตัดให้สั้นหรือปิด

questions.stem: How would you describe the tone of the notice?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ประกาศช่วยอำนวยความสะดวก (ขยายเวลา) และอวยพร "best of luck" จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงช่วยเหลือและให้กำลังใจ

readingShort.title: How to Set Up Your Student Email Account
readingShort.passage:
Follow these steps to set up your school email account before the first day of class.

1. Open your web browser and go to the school website.
2. Click the "Student Login" button at the top right of the page.
3. Enter the username printed on your registration card. Your first password is your date of birth in the form DDMMYYYY.
4. The system will then ask you to create a new password. Choose one that is at least eight characters long and includes a number.
5. Once you are logged in, read the "Welcome" message, which contains your class timetable.

If you forget your password, do not create a second account. Instead, visit the IT office in Room 105 with your student card, and a staff member will reset it for you.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this text?
options: To explain how to repair a broken school computer.
To explain how to set up a student email account.
To explain how to register for brand-new classes.
To explain how to contact a teacher by email.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ข้อความเป็นขั้นตอนการตั้งค่าอีเมลนักเรียน (set up your school email account) ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ตรงกับเนื้อหา

questions.stem: What is a student's first password?
options: The username printed on the registration card.
A word chosen freely by the student in advance.
A secret code sent later by the IT office staff.
The student's date of birth in DDMMYYYY form.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ขั้นที่ 3 ระบุว่ารหัสผ่านแรกคือวันเกิดรูปแบบ DDMMYYYY ส่วน username ใช้ล็อกอิน ไม่ใช่รหัสผ่าน

questions.stem: What must the new password include?
options: At least eight characters and a number.
At least four letters and one symbol.
The student's full name and birth year.
A capital letter and the word school.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ขั้นที่ 4 บอกว่ารหัสใหม่ต้องยาวอย่างน้อย 8 ตัวอักษรและมีตัวเลข ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ตรงเงื่อนไข

questions.stem: What should a student do if they forget the password?
options: Quickly create a brand-new second account.
Send an email straight to the headmaster.
Visit the IT office with a student card.
Wait for the system to reset it on its own.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ข้อความบอกว่าอย่าสร้างบัญชีที่สอง ให้ไปที่ IT office ห้อง 105 พร้อมบัตรนักเรียน ตัวเลือกอื่นขัดกับคำแนะนำ

questions.stem: In the text, the word "reset" is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — "reset the password" คือตั้งรหัสใหม่ให้ ตรงกับ "to set again" ไม่ใช่ลบทิ้ง พิมพ์ หรือล็อก

questions.stem: How is the information mainly organized?
options: As a list of rules in order of importance.
As a comparison between two email systems.
As a story about one student's first day.
As a series of steps in the order to follow.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ข้อความเรียงเป็นขั้นตอน 1–5 ตามลำดับที่ต้องทำ จึงเป็นการจัดเรียงแบบขั้นตอน ไม่ใช่เปรียบเทียบหรือเล่าเรื่อง

readingShort.title: The Rise of Urban Farming
readingShort.passage:
In cities around the world, a quiet change is taking place on rooftops, balconies, and empty plots of land. Urban farming — growing food within a city rather than in the countryside — is becoming increasingly popular, and for several practical reasons.

The first is freshness. Vegetables grown in the city can reach local markets within hours, rather than travelling long distances by truck. This not only improves their taste but also reduces the fuel used in transport.

Urban farms also make use of space that would otherwise be wasted. A flat rooftop, for example, can be turned into a productive garden that helps cool the building below. Some restaurants now grow herbs on their own roofs, picking them only minutes before they are served.

There are social benefits too. Community gardens, where neighbours share a plot of land, bring people together and give city children a chance to see how food is grown. For many families, this connection to nature is a welcome break from busy urban life.

Of course, urban farming cannot replace traditional agriculture; cities simply do not have enough space to feed everyone. But as a supplement, it offers fresher food, greener buildings, and stronger communities — a small but meaningful step toward more sustainable cities.

questions.stem: What is the passage mainly about?
options: Why traditional farming is no longer necessary.
How to grow vegetables on a small balcony.
The benefits of farming inside modern cities.
The long history of farming in the countryside.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — บทเล่าข้อดีของการทำเกษตรในเมือง (urban farming) ส่วน A ขัดกับย่อหน้าสุดท้าย, B/D เป็นรายละเอียดหรือไม่ใช่ใจความหลัก

questions.stem: According to the passage, why does city-grown food taste better?
options: It reaches local markets within just a few hours.
It is grown using special chemical fertilisers.
It is always sold at much cheaper market prices.
It is kept in cold storage for several days first.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 ระบุว่าผักในเมืองถึงตลาดภายในไม่กี่ชั่วโมง ทำให้รสชาติดีขึ้น ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่มีในบท

questions.stem: How can a rooftop garden help the building below it?
options: By providing several extra rooms for residents.
By helping to keep the building cooler.
By blocking all the noise from the street.
By producing electricity from the sunlight.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 บอกว่าสวนบนดาดฟ้า "helps cool the building below" ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ได้กล่าวถึง

questions.stem: In the last paragraph, the word "supplement" is closest in meaning to:
options: a strict rule that must be obeyed
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — "as a supplement" หมายถึงสิ่งที่เสริมเข้าไป ตรงกับ "an extra addition" บริบทคือเกษตรในเมืองเป็นส่วนเสริม ไม่ใช่แทนที่เกษตรปกติ

questions.stem: In paragraph 4, "this connection" refers to:
options: the link between two separate city farms
the bond formed between teachers and pupils
city children seeing how food is grown
the route that delivery trucks take daily
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ประโยคก่อนหน้าพูดถึงเด็กในเมืองได้เห็นว่าอาหารปลูกอย่างไร ดังนั้น this connection to nature จึงหมายถึงการได้สัมผัสธรรมชาติผ่านการเห็นการปลูกอาหาร

questions.stem: What does the writer suggest about urban farming?
options: It is useful but cannot feed a whole city alone.
It will soon completely replace normal farms.
It is far too expensive for most cities to try.
It can only ever work in very small villages.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าสุดท้ายบอกว่ามันแทนเกษตรดั้งเดิมไม่ได้ แต่เป็นส่วนเสริมที่ดี จึงอนุมานได้ว่ามีประโยชน์แต่เลี้ยงคนทั้งเมืองลำพังไม่ได้

questions.stem: What is the writer's attitude toward urban farming?
options: Positive but realistic about its limits.
Confused about how it really works.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ผู้เขียนชมข้อดีหลายอย่างแต่ยอมรับข้อจำกัด (แทนเกษตรปกติไม่ได้) จึงเป็นทัศนคติเชิงบวกแต่สมจริง

readingShort.title: Should Students Use Smartphones in Class?
readingShort.passage:
Few topics divide teachers and students as sharply as the use of smartphones in the classroom. Some schools ban them completely, while others encourage their use as learning tools. So which side is right?

Those who oppose phones in class make a strong case. Smartphones are a constant source of distraction: a single notification can pull a student's attention away from a lesson, and research suggests that even having a phone on the desk can reduce concentration. There is also the temptation to cheat, or simply to scroll through social media instead of listening.

Supporters, however, argue that banning phones ignores their real value. A smartphone can be a dictionary, a calculator, and a gateway to information all at once. Used well, it allows students to look up unfamiliar words, take part in online quizzes, and learn at their own pace.

The truth, perhaps, lies somewhere in between. The problem is not the device itself but how it is used. Rather than banning phones outright, schools might teach students to use them responsibly — switching them to silent during explanations, and taking them out only for specific tasks. In a world where technology is everywhere, learning to manage it may prove more useful than learning to live without it.

questions.stem: What is the main question the writer explores?
options: Whether smartphones are too costly for students.
Whether teachers should be allowed to use phones.
Whether social media is harmful to young people.
Whether students should use smartphones in class.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ทั้งบทถกประเด็น "ควรให้นักเรียนใช้สมาร์ตโฟนในห้องเรียนไหม" ตัวเลือกอื่นเป็นประเด็นข้างเคียงที่ไม่ใช่คำถามหลัก

Phones help students learn at their own pace.

questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ฝ่ายสนับสนุนบอกว่าโทรศัพท์เป็นทั้งพจนานุกรม เครื่องคิดเลข และประตูสู่ข้อมูล ตัวเลือกอื่นเกินจริงหรือไม่มีในบท

options: It lists steps for using a phone in class.
It tells one student's story about a phone.

readingShort.title: Letter: A Reply to a Customer Complaint
readingShort.passage:
Dear Ms. Chen,

Thank you for your letter of 3 May regarding the laptop bag you purchased from our online store. I am sorry to hear that the zip broke after only two weeks, and I understand how frustrating this must be.

Having looked into your case, I can confirm that your purchase is still covered by our one-year warranty. We would therefore like to offer you two options. You may either return the faulty bag for a full refund, or we can send you a replacement free of charge. In both cases, we will cover the cost of postage.

To proceed, simply reply to this letter and let us know which option you prefer. If you choose a replacement, please confirm your delivery address so that we can send the new bag without delay.

Once again, please accept our apologies for the inconvenience. We value you as a customer and hope to serve you better in the future.

Yours sincerely,
David Lim
Customer Service Manager

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this letter?
options: To advertise a brand-new range of laptop bags.
To respond to a complaint and offer some options.
To ask the customer to pay for a repair service.
To explain why the warranty has already expired.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — จดหมายตอบข้อร้องเรียนเรื่องกระเป๋าเสียและเสนอทางเลือก (คืนเงินหรือเปลี่ยนใหม่) ตัวเลือกอื่นขัดกับเนื้อหา

questions.stem: What two options does the company offer Ms. Chen?
options: A discount voucher or a written apology.
A repair in store or a longer warranty.
A full refund or a free replacement bag.
A cash reward or a free laptop computer.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — จดหมายเสนอ "a full refund" หรือ "a replacement free of charge" ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ตรงกับข้อเสนอ

questions.stem: Who will pay for the cost of postage?
options: The company will cover the postage cost.
Ms. Chen must pay the postage herself.
The postage will be split between them.
No postage is needed for either option.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — จดหมายระบุ "we will cover the cost of postage" จึงเป็นบริษัทที่จ่ายค่าส่ง

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the company?
options: It refuses to take responsibility for faults.
It no longer sells any kind of laptop bag.
It blames Ms. Chen for breaking the zip.
It wants to keep the customer satisfied.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — บริษัทขอโทษ เสนอทางเลือก และบอกว่า "value you as a customer" จึงอนุมานได้ว่าต้องการรักษาลูกค้าให้พอใจ

questions.stem: In the letter, the word "faulty" is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — "the faulty bag" หมายถึงกระเป๋าที่ชำรุด/ใช้ไม่ได้ ตรงกับ "broken or not working" บริบทคือซิปเสีย

questions.stem: How would you describe the tone of the letter?
options: Rude and dismissive of the customer.
Casual and full of friendly jokes.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — จดหมายใช้ภาษาสุภาพ ขอโทษ และเป็นทางการ (Dear / Yours sincerely / accept our apologies) จึงเป็นน้ำเสียงสุภาพและเป็นมืออาชีพ

readingShort.title: How Music Shapes the Brain
readingShort.passage:
Most of us listen to music every day — on the way to school, while studying, or simply to relax — without thinking much about what it actually does to us. Yet over the past few decades, scientists have discovered that music is far more than entertainment. It affects the human brain in remarkable ways, shaping our emotions, our memory, our bodies, and even the way we learn.

Perhaps the most obvious effect of music is on our mood. A fast, upbeat song can make us feel energetic, while a slow, gentle melody can calm us down or bring back distant memories. This happens because music triggers the release of chemicals in the brain that are linked to pleasure and reward — the same chemicals involved when we eat a favourite food or receive good news. This is why a single song can sometimes change the way we feel in a matter of seconds.

Music also has a surprisingly powerful connection to memory. Many people find it far easier to remember information when it is set to a tune; this is exactly why young children learn the alphabet through song. Researchers have even found that patients who have lost much of their memory through illness can still recall songs from their youth, long after most other memories have faded. The rhythm and melody seem to provide a kind of map that helps the brain store and later recover information.

Beyond thoughts and feelings, music can even influence the body. Studies have shown that listening to a steady beat can help people walk more smoothly, which is why music is now used to support patients recovering from injuries that affect movement. Hospitals sometimes play calming music to lower patients' stress before an operation, and athletes rely on energetic playlists to push themselves further during training. The body, it seems, responds naturally to rhythm, often without our being aware of it.

Given all these benefits, it is tempting to assume that music always helps us study. The truth, however, is more complicated. Quiet background music without words may help some people concentrate, but loud music with lyrics often does the opposite, competing for the very attention that the task requires. Whether music helps or harms, in other words, depends on the person, the type of music, and the kind of work being done.

What is clear is that music is deeply woven into the way our minds and bodies work. It can lift our spirits, unlock distant memories, ease pain, and, used wisely, support our learning. The next time you put on your headphones, it may be worth remembering that you are not simply hearing a song — you are giving your brain a gentle but genuine workout.

questions.stem: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How to choose the best music for studying hard.
The many ways music affects the brain and body.
Why most young children enjoy singing the alphabet song.
The history of music in hospitals and sport.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — บทเล่าผลของดนตรีต่อสมองและร่างกายหลายด้าน (อารมณ์ ความจำ ร่างกาย การเรียน) ส่วน A/C/D เป็นรายละเอียดย่อย

questions.stem: Why can a song change how we feel so quickly?
options: Because it forces the body to move in rhythm.
Because it slowly improves our long-term memory over the years.
Because it blocks out all other nearby sounds.
Because it triggers brain chemicals linked to pleasure.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ย่อหน้าที่ 2 บอกว่าดนตรีกระตุ้นการหลั่งสารเคมีในสมองที่เชื่อมกับความสุข ทำให้อารมณ์เปลี่ยนได้ในไม่กี่วินาที

questions.stem: What does the passage say about music and memory?
options: Information set to a tune is often easier to recall.
Music erases painful memories from early childhood.
Only trained musicians can remember songs clearly.
Songs are harder to remember than spoken words.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ย่อหน้าที่ 3 ระบุว่าข้อมูลที่ใส่ทำนองจะจำง่ายขึ้น (เด็กเรียน ABC ผ่านเพลง) ตัวเลือกอื่นขัดกับบท

questions.stem: How is music used to help the body, according to the passage?
options: It is used to help patients fall fast asleep.
It is used to teach young children how to read printed music.
It is used to help patients recovering movement.
It is used to test how fast athletes can run.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ย่อหน้าที่ 4 บอกว่าจังหวะช่วยให้เดินได้ราบรื่นขึ้น จึงใช้ช่วยผู้ป่วยที่ฟื้นฟูการเคลื่อนไหว ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ตรง

questions.stem: In paragraph 5, the word "competing" is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — "competing for the attention" หมายถึงแย่งความสนใจ ตรงกับ "struggling to take" บริบทคือเพลงดังมีเนื้อร้องไปแย่งสมาธิที่งานต้องใช้

questions.stem: What can be inferred about playing loud music with lyrics while studying?
options: It always helps every student concentrate better.
It is the best way to remember new information.
It has no effect at all on how we work.
It may make it harder to focus on the task.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ย่อหน้าที่ 5 บอกว่าเพลงดังมีเนื้อร้องมักไปแย่งสมาธิ จึงอนุมานได้ว่าอาจทำให้โฟกัสงานยากขึ้น

questions.stem: In paragraph 2, "the same chemicals" refers to chemicals involved when we:
options: eat a favourite food or hear good news
listen to a slow and gentle melody
lose our memory through a long illness

questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ประโยคนั้นเทียบว่าเป็นสารเคมีชุดเดียวกับตอน "eat a favourite food or receive good news" จึงตอบข้อ A

questions.stem: What is the writer's main purpose?
options: To warn readers about the dangers of loud music.
To advertise a new brand of music headphones.
To explain how music affects the mind and body.
To prove that music is better than any medicine.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — บทอธิบายผลของดนตรีต่อจิตใจและร่างกายอย่างเป็นกลาง ไม่ได้เตือนภัย โฆษณา หรือพิสูจน์ว่าดีกว่ายา

questions.stem: How is the passage mainly organized?
options: By telling one patient's story from start to end.
By describing several different effects of music.

questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — บทไล่อธิบายผลของดนตรีทีละด้าน (อารมณ์ → ความจำ → ร่างกาย → การเรียน) จึงเป็นการบรรยายหลายผลกระทบ

readingShort.title: Notice: New Library Opening Hours
readingShort.passage:
NOTICE TO ALL STUDENTS
Riverside School Library

Starting Monday, 2 June, the school library will open at new times. From Monday to Friday, the library will be open from 7:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. On Saturdays, it will open from 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. The library will be closed on Sundays and public holidays.

Students may borrow up to three books at a time for two weeks. If you return a book late, you must pay a fine of five baht per day. Reference books and magazines cannot be taken out of the library, but you may read them at the study tables.

Please remember that the library is a quiet space. Mobile phones must be switched to silent mode, and eating is not allowed inside. Students who need help finding a book can ask Ms. Suda at the front desk.

We hope you enjoy using the library this term.
The Library Team

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?
options: To inform students about the library's new opening hours and rules.
To invite students to a book sale at the library.
To announce that a new library building will open soon.
To report the results of a reading competition.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ประกาศแจ้งเวลาเปิดใหม่และกฎการใช้ห้องสมุด ตัวเลือกอื่นไม่ได้กล่าวถึงในประกาศ

questions.stem: When is the library closed?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ข้อความระบุชัด: closed on Sundays and public holidays

questions.stem: How long can a student keep a borrowed book?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — borrow up to three books ... for two weeks

questions.stem: In paragraph 2, the word "fine" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — บริบท 'pay a fine of five baht per day' = ค่าปรับ (เงินที่ต้องจ่ายเป็นบทลงโทษ)

questions.stem: What can be inferred about reference books and magazines?
options: They are cheaper than other books.
They can only be used inside the library.
They are the most popular items.
They can be borrowed for one week.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — 'cannot be taken out ... but you may read them at the study tables' → ใช้ได้เฉพาะในห้องสมุด

questions.stem: What is the overall tone of the notice?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — ให้ข้อมูลพร้อมขอความร่วมมืออย่างสุภาพ ('Please remember...', 'We hope you enjoy')

readingShort.title: Email: Science Fair Volunteers Needed
readingShort.passage:
From: Mr. Adisak Boonmee, Science Department
To: All Grade 11 Students
Subject: Volunteers Needed for the Science Fair

Dear students,

Our school will hold its annual Science Fair on Friday, 18 July, in the main hall. This year's theme is 'Science for a Better Future.' We are looking for student volunteers to help make the event a success.

Volunteers will help in three ways. Some will guide visitors around the hall and explain the projects. Others will help set up tables and posters on Thursday afternoon. A few volunteers will take photographs during the fair.

If you would like to join, please write your name on the sign-up sheet outside the science room before Wednesday, 9 July. Volunteers must attend a short meeting on 15 July at 3:30 p.m. to receive their tasks.

This is a great chance to practice your English and meet students from other schools who will visit us. We hope many of you will take part.

Best wishes,
Mr. Adisak

questions.stem: Why did Mr. Adisak write this email?
options: To ask students to volunteer at the Science Fair.
To cancel this year's Science Fair.
To invite parents to visit the school.
To announce the winners of the Science Fair.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — อีเมลเชิญชวนนักเรียนมาเป็นอาสาสมัครช่วยงาน Science Fair

questions.stem: What is this year's theme?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — ระบุชัด: This year's theme is 'Science for a Better Future.'

questions.stem: What must a student do to become a volunteer?
options: Pay a small fee at the science room.
Send an email to Mr. Adisak.
Write their name on the sign-up sheet before 9 July.
Come to the main hall on 18 July.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — 'write your name on the sign-up sheet outside the science room before Wednesday, 9 July'

questions.stem: What will some volunteers do on Thursday afternoon?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — 'help set up tables and posters on Thursday afternoon'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the people who will visit the fair?
options: Some will come from other schools.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — 'meet students from other schools who will visit us' → มีผู้มาเยือนจากโรงเรียนอื่น

questions.stem: In the last paragraph, "take part" means
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — 'take part' = เข้าร่วม (ในกิจกรรม)

readingShort.title: Advertisement: Summer English Adventure Camp
readingShort.passage:
SUMMER ENGLISH ADVENTURE CAMP
Speak. Explore. Grow.

Are you ready to improve your English while having fun? Join our two-week Summer English Adventure Camp this April in Chiang Mai!

Our camp is designed for students aged 13 to 17 who want to build confidence in speaking English. Unlike ordinary classes, we use games, drama, and outdoor activities to help you learn naturally. All our teachers are experienced and come from around the world, so you will hear many different accents.

A typical day includes morning lessons, afternoon activities such as cooking or hiking, and evening group projects. At the end of the camp, each student gives a short presentation in English in front of friends and family.

The camp fee is 12,000 baht, which covers accommodation, meals, and all materials. Places are limited to forty students, so early booking is recommended. Students who register before 15 March receive a 10% discount.

To reserve your place, visit our website or call 02-555-1234. Don't miss this chance to make your English skills come alive!

To sell English textbooks to teenagers.

questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — 'covers accommodation, meals, and all materials'

questions.stem: How can students receive a discount?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — 'register before 15 March receive a 10% discount'

questions.stem: In the last sentence, "come alive" is closest in meaning to

questions.stem: Why does the advertisement mention that teachers come from around the world?
options: To explain why the camp is held in April.

readingShort.title: Why Breakfast Matters
readingShort.passage:
Many students skip breakfast because they wake up late or are not hungry in the morning. However, research shows that eating a good breakfast can have a strong effect on how well we learn.

When we sleep, our bodies use up the energy stored from the day before. By morning, the brain needs fresh fuel to work properly. Breakfast provides this fuel in the form of glucose, a type of sugar that the brain uses to concentrate and remember information. Students who eat breakfast tend to pay attention longer and perform better on tests than those who do not.

A healthy breakfast does not have to be large. A bowl of rice porridge, an egg, or some fruit with yoghurt can be enough. Foods that release energy slowly, such as whole grains, are especially useful because they keep us full until lunchtime. In contrast, sugary snacks give quick energy that disappears fast, leaving us tired and unable to focus.

Of course, breakfast alone will not turn a weak student into a top one. But combined with enough sleep and regular study, it is a simple habit that supports both the body and the mind.

questions.stem: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Rice porridge is the healthiest breakfast.
Sugar is the most dangerous food for the brain.
Students today sleep far too little.
Eating breakfast helps students concentrate and learn better.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ D — ใจความหลัก: อาหารเช้าช่วยให้นักเรียนมีสมาธิและเรียนรู้ได้ดีขึ้น

questions.stem: According to the passage, what does the brain use to concentrate?
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — 'glucose, a type of sugar that the brain uses to concentrate'

questions.stem: Why are whole grains a good breakfast choice?
options: They are very quick to cook.
They release energy slowly and keep us full.
They contain a lot of sugar.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — 'release energy slowly ... keep us full until lunchtime'

questions.stem: In paragraph 2, the word "fuel" refers to
options: energy for the body and brain.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ C — บริบทเปรียบ glucose เป็น 'fresh fuel' ของสมอง = พลังงาน

questions.stem: What does the writer suggest about sugary snacks?
options: They are better than whole grains.
They are not a good choice for staying focused.
They should be eaten every morning.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — 'quick energy that disappears fast, leaving us tired and unable to focus' → ไม่เหมาะถ้าต้องการมีสมาธิ

questions.stem: What does the writer say in the final paragraph?
options: Breakfast is more important than sleeping.
Breakfast works best together with enough sleep and regular study.
Students should stop eating sugary snacks completely.
Breakfast alone can make any student the best in class.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ B — 'combined with enough sleep and regular study...' และย้ำว่า 'will not turn a weak student into a top one'

questions.stem: What is the writer's main purpose?
options: To explain why breakfast is important for learning.
To sell a particular breakfast product.

readingShort.title: How to Apply for a Student Library Card Online
readingShort.passage:
The City Library now allows students to apply for a library card online. Follow these steps to get your card.

First, go to the City Library website and click on the 'Membership' button at the top of the page. You will be asked to create an account using your email address and a password. Make sure to choose a password that you can remember easily but others cannot guess.

Next, fill in the application form with your full name, date of birth, and home address. You will also need to upload a clear photo of your student ID card. Without this, your application cannot be processed.

After you submit the form, you will receive a confirmation email within two working days. This email contains a temporary membership number. Take this number, together with your real student ID, to any branch of the City Library to collect your card.

Please note that the online service is only for new members. If you have lost your old card, you must visit a branch in person to ask for a replacement.

questions.stem: What does the passage mainly explain?
options: Why the City Library is closing some branches.
How to replace a lost library card.
The rules for borrowing books from the library.
How to apply for a library card online.

readingShort.title: Letter: Request to Use the Community Hall
readingShort.passage:
45 Sukhumvit Road
Bangkok 10110
12 May 2026

The Manager
Greenfield Community Centre
Bangkok

Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing on behalf of the Riverside School English Club to request the use of your main hall on Saturday, 28 June. We would like to hold a one-day English speaking contest for around eighty students from several local schools.

The event would run from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. We would need the hall, thirty chairs, and a sound system with two microphones. Our teachers and student volunteers will arrive at 7:30 a.m. to prepare the room, and we will make sure everything is cleaned and returned to its place by 5:00 p.m.

I understand that the centre usually charges a fee for using the hall. Could you please let me know the cost and whether any discount is available for school events? We would also be grateful if you could confirm that the date is free.

Thank you for considering our request. I look forward to your reply.

Yours faithfully,
Nattaya Sombat
President, English Club

GRADE 10 FIELD TRIP — SCIENCE MUSEUM
Saturday, 5 July

Please read the programme carefully and arrive on time.

7:00 a.m. – Meet at the school car park. Check your name with your teacher.

readingShort.title: Should Schools Start Later?
readingShort.passage:
Every morning, millions of teenagers drag themselves out of bed before sunrise to get to school on time. Many arrive tired and find it hard to concentrate in their first lessons. Some experts now argue that schools should start later in the day, and there are good reasons to take this idea seriously.

Scientists have found that during the teenage years, the body's natural clock changes. Teenagers tend to feel sleepy later at night and need about nine hours of sleep. When school starts at 7:30 or 8:00 a.m., most students simply cannot get enough rest. As a result, they may feel moody, forget information, and even fall ill more often.

Schools that have moved their start time to 9:00 a.m. report encouraging results. Students are more alert, attendance improves, and fewer pupils fall asleep in class. Teachers also notice that lessons run more smoothly when learners are awake and ready.

Of course, a later start is not always easy to arrange. Parents may need to leave for work early, and after-school activities could finish in the dark. Still, the benefits for students' health and learning are hard to ignore. Perhaps it is time for more schools to rethink the school day.

questions.stem: What is the main argument of the article?
options: Schools should consider starting later so teenagers can get enough sleep.
Teachers need more rest than students do.
Teenagers should simply go to bed much earlier.
The school day is far too long for everyone.
questions.explanation_th: ตอบข้อ A — ใจความหลัก: โรงเรียนควรพิจารณาเริ่มเรียนสายขึ้นเพื่อให้วัยรุ่นนอนพอ

readingShort.title: App Review: StudyTime
readingShort.passage:
If you often forget to do your homework, the free app StudyTime might help. I have used it for two months, and overall I am happy with it.

The main feature is a simple planner. You type in your tasks and the time they are due, and the app sends you a reminder before each deadline. You can also tick off tasks when they are finished, which feels satisfying. The design is clean and easy to use, even for younger students.

StudyTime also has a 'focus mode'. When you turn it on, the app blocks games and social media for a chosen number of minutes. This really helped me study without checking my phone every few minutes.

The app is not perfect, though. It sometimes sends reminders late, and there are too many advertisements in the free version. To remove the ads, you have to pay 99 baht.

In short, StudyTime is a useful tool for busy students. I would give it four stars out of five.

questions.stem: What is the purpose of this text?
options: To advertise a paid online course.
To teach students how to do their homework.
To explain how to design a new app.
To give an opinion about a study app.

ANNUAL SPORTS DAY — ANNOUNCEMENT

This year's Sports Day will take place on Friday, 11 July, at the school stadium. All students from Grades 7 to 12 are expected to take part.

Students have been divided into four colour teams: Red, Blue, Green, and Yellow. You can find your team name on the lists posted outside the gym. Each team will compete in running races, football, and a fun tug-of-war.

readingShort.title: Notice: Updated Chemical Storage Protocol
readingShort.passage:
Effective October 1st, all research personnel must strictly adhere to the revised guidelines for handling volatile organic compounds (VOCs).

Firstly, secondary containment trays are now mandatory for all liquid VOCs stored outside the primary fume hoods. Failure to utilize these trays will result in an immediate suspension of laboratory privileges.

Secondly, the digital inventory log must be updated concurrently with any chemical withdrawal. The previous grace period of 24 hours for logging usage has been abolished to ensure real-time tracking of hazardous materials.

Finally, in the event of a minor spill (under 50 ml), personnel should deploy the neutralizer kits located adjacent to the emergency showers before contacting the hazardous materials team. For spills exceeding this volume, evacuate the vicinity immediately and trigger the central alarm.

These measures are implemented not to hinder your research, but to foster a secure environment for all facility occupants.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?
options: To enforce stricter regulations for handling dangerous chemicals.
To introduce a new hazardous materials team.
To ban the use of volatile organic compounds in the lab.
To explain how to use the emergency showers properly.
questions.explanation_th: ประกาศนี้ออกมาเพื่อบังคับใช้กฎการจัดเก็บ/ใช้งานสารเคมีที่เข้มงวดขึ้น ('must strictly adhere to the revised guidelines')

options: A temporary loss of their right to use the lab.
questions.explanation_th: 'immediate suspension of laboratory privileges' = ถูกพักสิทธิใช้ห้องแล็บชั่วคราว

questions.stem: According to the updated protocol, when must the digital inventory log be updated?
options: At the exact moment chemicals are taken out.
At the end of the working day.
Before bringing new chemicals into the building.
questions.explanation_th: 'updated concurrently with any chemical withdrawal' = ต้องลงบันทึกทันทีที่นำสารเคมีออกมา (24 ชม.เป็นกฎเก่าที่ถูกยกเลิกแล้ว — ตัวลวง)
